data.title: Want to know an easy hack for weight loss? (Portion size with palm)
data.description:
Want to know an easy hack for weight loss?


Just use your palm!


It is a common misunderstanding that the only key to weight loss is to eat foods that are healthy and low calorie.


This is where most people go wrong‼️


Well, the truth is that it's not just about eating healthy or low-calorie foods. There is another mantra that is important for weight loss and that is ‘PORTION SIZE’.


Increased portion sizes are thought to contribute excessive calories and unwanted weight gain. Even healthy binging is harmful to health!


The more you have on your plate, the more you eat & the more you gain!


??So here’s how you can use just your palm to understand portion size


1 serving of carbohydrates (cereals, millets)= 1 cupped hand


1 serving of veggies & salads = 1 fist


1 serving of fats (butter, ghee)= 1 thumb


1 serving of high protein foods (paneer, chicken, fish, tofu) = 1 palm


You need to be wise about your portion size!


That is why at Balance Nutrition we educate our clients about portion size with our BN exclusive Eat-In- Portions Guide which gives them a clear idea about how much to eat!

data.title: Cucumber sev puri
data.description:
You don't have to give up on chaat while dieting!


Try this cucumber sev puri! It is filled with protein, antioxidants and vitamins and fulfills your cravings without adding extra calories!


Replacing normal fried puri, sev and potato makes it a perfect low calorie evening party snack!


Recipe


Dried white peas - 10 g

Turmeric Powder - 1 pinch

Asafoetida - 1 pinch

Salt - to taste

Oil - ½ tsp

Green chutney - as required.

Sweet date and tamarind chutney - as required.

Cucumber [cut in round slices] - 1 no

Onion, finely chopped - ½ no.

Tomato, finely chopped - ½ no.

Chaat masala - ½ tsp

Lemon juice - 1 tsp

Fresh coriander [for garnish]

Baked sev [for garnish].


Step 1 :

Soaked dried peas in 1 cup water for about 6 hours.


Step 2 :

Drain and boil in 1 & ½ cup water with turmeric, asafoetida and salt till soft.


Step 3 :

Let the ragda cool down.


Step 4 :

Plate cucumber slices, add ragda, finely chopped onions, chaat masala, green chutney, tamarind, sweet dates chutney and squeezed lemon juice like a sev puri.


Step 5 :

Garnish with baked sev and coriander.


So next time you have a guest at your house or you feel like eating chaat, do serve this healthy sev puri and enjoy your snack!

data.title: Mango chia pudding
data.description:
If you are Constipated, add this High Fiber-Fruit Chia Pudding to your Breakfast thrice a week!


??Dietary fiber is an important nutrient that aids in digestion. Soaked chia seeds contains 40% fiber which brings them to the top of the list of fiber-rich food!


??The gel formed is due to mucilage and digestible fiber and helps in stool movements!


??It not only helps to relieve constipation but also in regulating body weight, blood sugar level & health of the colon!


But don’t forget to drink water throughout the whole day for this recipe to work well or it can cause you issues like bloating, diarrhea & stomach ache


Here’s how you can make it ?

Recipe:


*Milk of your choice- 100 ml

*Soaked Chia seeds - 1.5 tsp

*Mixed Fruits (avoid mango if diabetic) - 1 katori

*Almonds/Walnuts- 2-3 pieces

*Cinnamon Powder - a pinch


Step 1:

Mix milk and soaked Chia seeds in a bowl


Step 2 :

Store it in the refrigerator for an hour


Step 3 :

Top it with cinnamon, fresh fruit & nuts of your choice!

data.title: Pomegranate raita
data.description:
High Protein Weight loss Nashta under 150 calories!


INGREDIENTS:

• Chilled yogurt

• Fresh pomegranate

• Roasted cumin powder

• Black pepper powder

DIRECTIONS:

data.title: Did you know that high-calorie snacks can sneakily lead to obesity ⁉️
data.description:
nutritionist_khyatirupani


 57w

Did you know that high-calorie snacks can sneakily lead to obesity ⁉️


Nuts like almonds may be packed with fiber and omega-3 fatty acids, but here's the twist – they're calorie-dense too!


Eating just a handful might not keep hunger at bay, and you could end up munching on more than you had planned!


Here's a simple solution to this : Mix 3-4 almonds with some kurmura or pair them with a juicy fruit, for a high-volume, low-calorie snack that'll leave you feeling full till your next meal!


Don't just eat right but also eat smart!

data.title: Tried losing weight, but still stuck?
data.description:

Here is where things go wrong-

*WHAT you place on your plate reflects your body size’ should be rewritten as WHAT and HOW MUCH you place on your plate reflects on your waist!

data.title: Want to know an easy hack for weight loss?
data.video: [{"file":{"path":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=980tF-LZuFs","name":"Want to know an easy hack for weight loss?","type":"video/mp4"}}]
data.description:
This easy hack will give you long term sustainable results

A SMALL PORTION DOES NOT EQUAL LESS CALORIES.


Coconut, a very important part of many dishes in India, is extremely high in calories that come from fat. On the other hand, a big bowl of strawberries are only 65 kcal.

Now, imagine if you eat 50 g coconut which you do while eating a Dosa. you are adding almost 250 calories (all from fat) to your diet.


This not only leads to weight gain but also risks our cardiac function.


Does that mean you have to stay away from coconut? Not at all!


You can easily have 3 tbsp of Coconut Chutney once a week without any harm.

If you are a South Indian, up to 5 tsp of coconut in your diet each week should not pose a problem ?

data.title: Are you bloated?
data.description:
Here"s how to manage it

Get moving.

Exercising = ditch the water weight & reduce body inflammation.


Drink up.

More water = elimination of extra fluids and sodium. Aim for two to three liters of water a day.


Pump up the potassium.

Potassium helps moderate many important bodily functions, including blood pressure and water balance. Potassium-rich foods include bananas, pumpkins, plantains, and papaya.


Feed yourself fiber.

A healthy digestive system is the best way to avoid water weight because it means everything is in balance.

data.title: Do you have diabetes ?
data.description:
Recognizing 3 or more of these signs? It might be a signal of diabetes:


-Increased thirst

-Frequent urination

-Unexplained weight loss

-Fatigue

-Blurred vision

-Slow-healing wounds or sores

-Constant hunger

-Tingling or numbness in hands or feet

-Recurrent infections

-Changes in skin color or texture


Stay tuned for valuable insights on managing diabetes and leading a healthier lifestyle. Your well-being matters!

data.title: Fruit cravings restricted by weight ?
data.description:
Heres what you need to know


Weight loss is not just about the number of calories you consume in a day

To lose weight & sustain your diet for the long term, you have to focus on the foods that are HIGH in Volume & Low in Calories.


Foods that are high Volume & Low Calories:

1. Watermelon (250g): 75 calories

2. Berries (1 cup): 80 calories

3. 1 bowl Clear Veg Soup: 50 calories

4. 1 bowl Kurmura: 56 calories

5. 1 bowl Saute cabbage: 65 calories

6. 1 glass buttermilk: 103 calories


These kinds of foods are very helpful when added to your main meals or snacks as they keep you FULL making it easy to lose weight.


Foods that are Low Volume High Calories:

1. Coconut Chutney (2 tbsp): 105 calories

2. Flax/Pumpkin seeds: 80 calories

3. Paneer (100g): 265 calories

4. Nuts (100g): 650 calories

5. Dried Fruits: 570 calories


These foods can be called healthy but are not apt for weight loss as they are low in volume & have very many calories.

You will end up feeling hungry & not see any loss on the scale.

data.title: Can a diabetic have coconut water ?
data.description:
Myth or fact: let us find out.

Most of us think coconut water will have an impact on blood sugars.


Here are some facts-


1. Coconut water has a low glycemic index and contains a very low amount of natural sugar which doesn’t lead to a spike in your glucose levels.

2. With low sugar content, coconut water is suitable for people with diabetes.

3. Coconut water helps control blood sugar thanks to its high content of potassium, manganese, magnesium, vitamin C, and L - arginine.


If you are thirsty& craving cola or juice, this is a far better alternative.

Be sure to choose an unsweetened variety and limit your intake to 1–2 cups (240–280 ml) per day.

For uncontrolled & very high blood sugar, add 1 tsp of chia seeds to the coconut water, let them swell up for 30 min, add a pinch of cinnamon powder & have.

data.title: Thyroid and weight loss
data.description:
All you need to know

The thyroid is a vital hormone, located in the front part of the neck below the voice box.The thyroid plays various roles such as controlling the rate of use of energy sources, and protein synthesis & controls the body"s sensitivity to other hormones.

The thyroid produces two hormones:
-Triiodothyronine or T3
-Tetra iodothyronine, also called as thyroxine or T4.

Subtle changes in these hormone secretions result in the occurrence of thyroid symptoms, which may create severe thyroid problems if proper initiative is not taken at the initial stage.

data.title: Invisible calories
data.description:
How to stay away from them

Calories matter - But the type of Calories matters too!


Most of us want to talk about calories in a particular food item or a dish. What we miss here is the purpose of counting the calories & our goals.


When you want to gain weight, the VOLUME of the Calories matter. As seen in the image above, 1 tsp of oil here is very low in volume & will barely fill your stomach. This will want you to eat more INCREASING your calorie load.


A simple glass of juice or a frappe can add a whooping 400 calories to our daily intake! This is more than the calories in a double egg ? omelet :)


The smart thing would be to stay away from consuming liquid or low-volume calories without keeping a tab.

data.title: Food volume
data.description:
How it is affecting your appetite

Food Volume & Weight!


Calories come from nutrients like carbohydrates, sugars, starch, fats & proteins.

Once we digest our food, the calories enter our body & utilized or then stored as fat.


When we lose weight, we create a calorie deficit, either by cutting calories from the diet or then exercising.

Similarly for gaining weight, calorie surplus is essential.


Certain foods when consumed need more calories to be digested by the body than they actually have.

These are NEGATIVE CALORIES.

Cabbage, broccoli, asparagus, apples & a lot of other vegetables & fruits fall in this category.

data.title: Weight is stuck ?
data.description:

Here is where things go wrong-

*WHAT you place on your plate reflects your body size’ should be rewritten as WHAT and HOW MUCH you place on your plate reflects on your waist!

*Think twice before UPSIZING your meal, it may save you some money but it will certainly add a few pounds to the body.

*Frequent headaches

data.title: Oats or rice ?
data.description:
The biggest fear in weight loss - WHITE RICE.

Let us understand more about weight loss foods & how they have an impact on the weighing scale.


*WHITE RICE - White polished rice has negligible fiber, high in starch & all the nutritious minerals are gone during polishing. The excess starch unused by your body is stored as glucose and later on fat.

*GRANOLA: This is made using oats & other whole grains. It is loaded with honey and dried fruits- high in calories & natural sugars.


SO WHAT IS BETTER?

White rice has fewer calories & if eaten in the same amounts as granola, you will be in a calorie deficit easily. So, anyone who wants to lose weight or wants to maintain their weight has to avoid granola.

Always ensure you maintain PORTION CONTROL!

data.title: Nuts has ZERO cholesterol.
data.description:

FACTS:
* Nuts have 0 cholesterol but are high in calories & also fat.
* A single serving of nuts therefore must not be more than 2 to 3 pieces at a time.

CONCLUSION:

data.title: This common kitchen ingredient is great for weight loss!
data.video: [{"file":{"path":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CrZ1YpCkIfg","name":"This common kitchen ingredient is great for weight loss!","type":"video/mp4"}}]
data.description:
Check it out

Cabbage & weight loss!


Even though cabbage is very low in calories, it has an impressive nutrient profile.

In fact, just 1 cup (89 grams) of raw green cabbage contains:

*Calories: 22

*Protein: 1 gram

*Fiber: 2 grams

*Vitamin K: 85% of the RDI

*Vitamin C: 54% of the RDI

*Folate: 10% of the RDI

*Manganese: 7% of the RDI

*Vitamin B6: 6% of the RDI

*Calcium: 4% of the RDI

*Potassium: 4% of the RDI

*Magnesium: 3% of the RDI


Cabbage also contains small amounts of other micronutrients, including vitamin A, iron & riboflavin.

As you can see in the list above, it is rich in vitamin B6 and folate, both of which are essential for many important processes in the body, including energy metabolism and the normal functioning of the nervous system.


In addition, cabbage is high in fiber and contains powerful antioxidants, including polyphenols and sulfur compounds.

data.title: Things you should no longer do!
data.description:
Follow this for a healthy lifestyle

Throw away these things from your kitchen:

Ketchup: Ketchup is one condiment that seems to enter our food unknowingly. I have seen children having all their meals only with ketchup. It is high in sugar, preservatives and should not be consumed. Healthier alternatives include chutneys made from tamarind and dates. You will get the same taste.

Sugar-Free Sweeteners: Sugar-free Indian sweets are made using dates and anjeer (figs), both of which are very high in natural sugars as well as calories. Artificial sweeteners when added to a dessert do not make it healthy or calorie free. If you crave a dessert, you must try the two bite rule.

“Healthy” Biscuits: Even the so called 'healthy' biscuits that claim to be sugar-free or low-fat, often have surprising amount of additives, hidden sugars and empty calories.

Soup Diets: Not just soup diet but any other diets that claim to give good results very quickly should be off your list. Weight loss happens only when you practically make changes to your diet.

Curry Leaves: Beneficial for your skin, hair, and overall health. Rich in vitamin A, vitamin B, vitamin C, vitamin B2, calcium & iron.
Chewing or eating curry leaves regularly helps in burning of calories & avoiding fat accumulation. Curry leaves contain Mahanimbine, an alkaloid that has anti-obesity and lipid-lowering effects.

Curcumin (Haldi): Suppresses fat tissue growth. It helps in losing weight by regulating sugar levels & preventing insulin resistance.

Til: Rich in mono unsaturated fatty acid, improves the good cholesterol. It also lowers blood glucose level and improves insulin sensitivity.

Lemongrass Tea: Kick-starts your metabolism & helps lose weight. This tea also helps in detoxification. Replace your soft drinks with lemongrass tea for effective weight loss. It is also low in calories.

data.title: The Japanese have the lowest rates of obesity & and diseases.

data.description:
You know why?

The Japanese have the lowest rates of obesity & and diseases.

1. A traditional Japanese diet is based on whole foods and rich in fish, seaweed and plant-based foods. They do not eat processed foods.

2. It consists of small portions of simple, fresh, and seasonal ingredients.

3.The use of chopsticks also reduces large amounts of food ingestion.

4. Hot green tea or cold barley tea are the beverages of choice, while alcoholic drinks like beer and sake are rare. So no snacking on junk.

5. The traditional Japanese diet is naturally rich in various nutrients including fiber, calcium, potassium, magnesium, iron, and vitamins A, C, and E & low in salts sugar and fat.

Thus we can certainly start following their dietary guidelines.

data.title: Nut Milk VS Regular Milk.
data.description:
Which one is better?

Nut Milk vs Regular Milk: Which is Better?
Many of us are moving to nut milk & oat milk instead of regular animal milk that we have had for centuries.
Ever wondered about the calorie content & other parameters of your milk choices?
Let’s break it down & understand better.
In 100ml of cow’s milk, you’ll find approximately 42 calories, while the same amount of almond or soy milk contains around 40-50 calories. Surprised? Don’t be!
Most kinds of milk, except full-fat buffalo milk offer similar calorie counts.
Additionally, cow’s milk contains about 3.25g of fat and 12mg of cholesterol per 100ml, more or less similar to nut milk variations.
While nut milk boasts health benefits, be wary of added preservatives like carrageenan, guar gum, and xanthan gum.
Most nut milks are primarily water with nuts comprising only about 5% of the ingredients. Watch out for those additives.

Aspartame is one of the world’s most popular artificial sweeteners used in various diet cold drinks, desserts, cereals, chewing gum, ice cream, yoghurts and many more food products.
This sweetener became popular as it does not raise blood sugar levels, like sugar. Thus, it’s used in a lot of zero-sugar or sugar-free foods & beverages.
WHO has labelled this sweetener as a possible "carcinogen" (cancer-causing agent).

data.title: White rice makes you fat?
data.description:
Click to know.

White rice makes you fat?

Here is a detailed explanation:
White rice (polished) especially that is cooked in a rice cooker or a pressure cooker where excess water (starch) is not removed, will make you fat over time.
This is because it is high in starch which causes a quick spike in your blood sugar. The glucose enters the bloodstream quickly & the excess is stored in the liver which converts to glycogen & if unused gets converted to fat.
This is how you can include white rice into your diet without fearing weight gain:
1. Use semi-polished rice & throw the excess water out. This ensures, a good amount of starch is reduced.
2. Have this rice with a plate full of veggies & a quarter of the plate with a protein source like dal, pulses, or lean meats. This ensures a slower breakdown of starch & keeps you full for a longer period.

data.title: Cardio or Weights?
data.description:
Whic is better for you?

Cardio or weights? Which is the best exercise after all !


Any form of cardio like cycling, walking, jogging or swimming is called aerobic.

This means that it requires a lot of breathing and works directly by increasing the heart rate and basic metabolic them.

This is best for someone who has a high fat percentage and is overweight.

It is also a very good exercise for someone who struggles to climb 2 floors without panting, someone who wants to participate in a marathon or any other aerobic/cardio activity.

It is also a good alternative for senior citizens who have weak joints & weak stamina.

If you have to increase your muscle mass, bone mass and are not waiting too much, then waits becomes a good exercise.

Lifting weights is an anaerobic activity, meaning it does not require constant breathing and is focusing on developing muscles.

Khyati Rupani's take

When we consume any food, apart from calories...it has carbohydrates, fat, protein and other micronutrients. These nutrients are extremely important in providing us nourishment. .. 1g protein and carbohydrates give us 4 kcal and 1g fat 9 kcal.

There are some foods which have ONLY HARMFUL CALORIES and no other nutrition. The net result of making these foods a part of our daily diet will be weight gain, obesity, diabetes and heart ailments. These are called empty calories

SABUDANA, table sugar, aerated beverages, candies, candy floss are all empty calories

data.title: Facts about Carbs!
data.description:
Kahani carbs ki.

Carbs Ki Kahani

Most of us think their weight issues are only because of carbs! Roti & rice bandh karna hai bas!

A few of us even proudly say that they don't do carbs after sunset. This helps them lose weight.

FACTS:

Not getting enough carbs can be bad. Eating too many carbs is bad. What does a person do???‍?

Many people reduce carbohydrate intake to help them lose weight, which shouldn't be a concern at all!!!!

Carbohydrates are the most efficient fuel for the brain, the internal functions of the body as well as a very healthy source for energy.

Western diets swear by high protein as they always are meat-eaters. High fat in meats has harmful effects on cardiac health.

On the other hand, diets rich in fiber and complex carbs always prove to be safer, healthier, and have high satiety making one feel full for long.

So avoid simple sugars, potatoes, pressure-cooked white rice, refined flour, sago in case you want to lose weight and stick to oats, millets instead.

The right carbs are your friends in weight loss.

data.title: This is a sign for you.
data.description:
Click here.

We always believe the key to weight loss and disease management is to eat foods that are HEALTHY and LOW CALORIE.

But somehow it has not worked out well and we fail to achieve the desired results.

So what do we do?

Most of us give up on seeing little or no difference and get back to our old eating habits and lifestyle.

Well, it's not about just eating healthy low-calorie food. There is another 'mantra' that needs our attention and that is 'PORTION SIZE'.

A portion size means eating the right amount/quantity of food as per the requirement of the body to attain its ideal weight according to the height and body type. Here is where things go wrong.

As was said WHAT you place on your plate reflects your body size' should be rewritten as WHAT and HOW MUCH you place on your plate reflects on your waist!

Think twice before UPSIZING your meal, it may save you some money but it will certainly add a few pounds to the body.

data.title: What are negative calories?
data.description:
Click here to know.

We don't like to be associated with any kind of negative stuff in life apart from seeing the weighing scale ?

Khyati Rupani's take

We all get scared of the word calorie. Well, it's not that bad after all. It is more important to know the SOURCE of the calorie than freak out just by mentioning or reading the word calories on any food label.

Calories come from nutrients like carbohydrates, sugars, starch, fats and proteins. Once we digest our food, the calories enter our body and utilised or then stored as fat.

When we lose weight, we create a calorie deficit. Either by cutting calories from the diet or then exercises.

How about eating foods that help you burn calories!!

Yes! Certain foods when consumed need more calories to be digested by the body than they actually have.

These are NEGATIVE CALORIES.

Cabbage, broccoli, asparagus, apples and a lot of other vegetables and fruits fall in this category

But if you feel eating and apple pie or having apple juice, cream of broccoli is going to be negative calories, you are mistaken. These have to be eaten raw or steamed to get the best results.

They are rich in vitamins and minerals, keep you full longer and hence help you lose weight

data.title: Fiber? Blessing or Curse?

Fibre - Blessing or Curse?
Most of us dietitians swear by fibre & its benefits in weight loss, diabetes, thyroid management & many other health conditions.
Hence, most weight loss diet is high in fibre & contain foods like brown rice, millets, fruits & vegetables.
A human body requires 25 - 30g fibre in a day.
Benefits of fibre:
Lowers your cholesterol level
Helps control blood sugar levels
Aids in achieving a healthy weight
But given our current lifestyle, we barely manage to get in 15 g of fibre leading to a lot of weight gain & digestive issues.
Sometimes however some diet plans contain a lot more than 40 g of fibre & if had over a long time it can be harmful.
Hence, if your symptoms are persistent for more than three months, make sure you inform your dietitian about this & she will alter the amount of fibre for you.

To lose weight and manage diabetes, a high fibre diet along with foods rich in chromium always plays a good role.

data.title: Stop blaming potatos for diabetic weight gain!

Most of us blame the potato as a reason for weight gain, diabetes & other issues.

• The real culprits are recipes made out of it like chips, fries, aloo fry subjis and not the humble potato.

* Most of us consider it as a ‘vegetable’ but it is a carbohydrate source (like roti/ rice) hence has to be treated like one.


* Aloo subji with rice / roti or Pav bhaji is a double carb serving in one meal. Avoid if you are trying to lose weight or have uncontrolled blood sugars.


* Aloo peanut upvas subji ( less oil, boiled potatoes) with a bowl of fruits and katori of curd is a perfect meal. You don’t need a sabudana khichadi here.


* A bowl of Mexican soup with a serving of potato, grilled chicken with a serving of boiled potatoes etc are a healthier way to have the potato.

data.title: Know about your body type.
data.description:
Click here.

I want to be like Kareena / Katrina / Anushka!

We have a few clients who are always looking to reach a particular WEIGHT!

My take:

Bodyweight comprises bones, muscles, water, and fat. While many of us have heavier and broader bones, few of us have higher muscle and the remaining have high fat.

Our appearance differs on these parameters and not the number on the scale.

Rather than the weight only as a parameter, it is healthy and practical for your dietitian to also understand your NATIONALITY, ETHNIC BACKGROUND & BODY TYPE and plan diets accordingly!

This will ensure you look at YOUR best & Healthy.

3 BODY TYPES:

Ectomorphs are thin individuals characterized by smaller bone structures and thinner limbs. This profile is linked to a fast metabolic rate and high carbohydrate tolerance.

Mesomorphs have a medium-sized bone structure and athletic body, and if they’re active, they usually have a considerable amount of lean mass. This profile leads to a predisposition for muscle gain and the maintenance of lower body fat.

Endomorphs have a larger bone structure with higher amounts of total body mass and fat mass. They tend to be naturally less active.

Balance Nutrition not only understands the weight but also body type and plans your charts depending on the same.

data.title: This condiment will worsen your health!
data.description:
Throw this if you still have it.

Side effects of ketchup are often neglected by its users. Tomato ketchup is a sauce used as a condiment.

The main ingredients of tomato ketchup besides tomatoes are sugar, salt & preservatives.

Home-made ketchups also contain high amounts of salt & sugar making it a very unhealthy addition to our diet.

Let’s see the top side effects of ketchup:

Acid reflux:

Tomatoes are also rich in citric and malic acid. When too much of these substances accumulate in your stomach, it will cause heartburn or acid reflux.

Allergic reactions:

Consuming food that is rich in this compound leads to the development of rashes. It also causes allergic reactions.

Joint Pain:

It is rich in solanine. This is a compound that builds up calcium in the tissues. Excess amounts can cause inflammation and body pains.

Diarrhea:

Raw tomatoes are susceptible to salmonella contamination which causes diarrhea.

Kidney Stone:

Since the fruit is rich in oxalate and calcium, it is not easily broken down if you consume excessive amounts of these nutrients, possibly leading to the formation of kidney stones.

data.title: Fasting or Feasting?
data.description:
Choose better.

ARE YOU FASTING/FEASTING? :)

SABUDANA

It is very high in calories and all are empty calories, meaning they contain no other nutrient other than pure simple carbs. So best avoided during fasts .

KUTTU KA ATTA

Buckwheat's (kuttu) is rich in antioxidants. Buckwheat helps in cholesterol-lowering. It is a rich source of manganese, copper and magnesium.

HEALTHY RECIPES- chilla, roti/ paratha and also khichdi.

AMARANTH FLOUR/ RAJGIRE KA ATTA

Amaranth or rajgira means “immortal” or “everlasting” in Greek because it contains more than three times the average amount of calcium and is also high in iron.

Rajgira also reduces one’s risk of osteoporosis, because it has twice the amount of calcium as milk.

HEALTHY RECIPES - thepla, rajgira porridge are some fast recipe options made from this wonder grain.

MAKAHNE/ FOX NUTS

It is rich in proteins, antioxidants , magnesium and has anti ageing properties. It relieves aches and pains and fatigue.

Makhana can be popped and used as a filler, also can be used in kheer .

WATERCHESNUT FLOUR/ SINGHARE KA ATTA

It is rich in minerals is low in sodium. It is also a rich source of fibre . It has a good effect on hair and skin.

Singhare ka atta can be used to make dosa, porridge, and thalipeeth.

BARNYARD MILLET FLOUR/ SAMA KE CHAVAL

Millets are rich in B vitamins, calcium, iron, potassium, magnesium, zinc, also gluten-free and has low-GI (Glycemic index) thus millets are suitable for people allergies/intolerance of wheat. Also for diabetic, weight loss millets are excellent.

Sama ke chawal can be used to make kheer , upma , pulav and dosas.

SWEET POTATO

Sweet potatoes pack a powerful nutritional punch. They have got over 400% of your daily needs for vitamin A in one medium spud. Sweet potatoes are a great source of beta-carotene, a powerful antioxidant known to give orange vegetables and fruits their vibrant color, which is converted to vitamin A in the body

Sweet potato can be just boiled and eaten as a snack and also can be used to make tikkis.

data.title: Want to know an "easy hack" for weight loss?

1 serving of carbohydrates = 1 cupped hand

White Rice:

2) Milling & polishing removes the most of the fiber.

3) It is low in potassium & contains 50% less manganese and iron.

4) Increases the risk of type 2 diabetes & high cholesterol.

Brown Rice:

1) Excellent source of vitamin B1 & B3.

2) Good source of dietary fiber & contains potassium.

3) Fat present is heart friendly & is rich source of manganese.

4) Reduces risk of type 2 diabetes & controls blood cholesterol.

5) Helps in weight loss.

6) It is easier to cook, tastier and also keeps you full for a longer time.

data.title: Can we reuse leftover oil?
data.description:
It is a very common practice in all Indian households to reuse the extra oil that is left over after frying.


Over the last 15 years in my practice, I have been telling all our clients as well as on videos over social media how harmful this practice can be. 


 A detailed understanding of the health risks of reused or reheated oils is important. Not only homes, hotels, and outside eateries reheat the oils for deep frying. FSSAI, Food License Registration Services (THE INDIA DOCS) doesn’t have any regulation for reused cooking oils.


If the oil isn’t changed regularly during deep frying there is the formation of Total Polar Matter, which is not digestible. It can increase the risk of heart disease, and gastrointestinal disorders. 


The Total Polar Matter is a reliable international indicator of cooking oil degradation. TPM should be below 24-25 to ensure safe consumption. In our country, however, there are no laws or any testing methods that are implied by the regulatory bodies or the hotels.


Simply put, 

1. Oils that are used for frying once should not be reused for any purpose. 

2. Use just enough oil to fry & avoid wastage 

3. Ensure the oil you use at home while frying does not start emitting smoke.

4. Fry on low heat using the smallest burner & a small utensil.

data.title: Eating healthy but not losing weight??
data.description:
Is your weight not going down on the scale despite all your efforts of eating healthy & being on a diet?


There may be a lot of reasons why this is happening. Take a look at the possible reasons & see if you can relate to any of them. ?


1. Eating too less: When you starve yourself, your metabolism slows down & your body starts storing fat causing a gain in weight. You should eat a high-fibre, low-fat & moderate-protein diet to lose weight.


2. Not enough protein: 1g of protein has 5.5 calories but to metabolize them, we burn 1.5 calories. This gives us 4 cals per g protein. Adding protein to the diet increases your metabolism. 


3. Not being active physically: For every hour you sit, you must ensure to walk for 10 min before you sit for another hour. During the day, ensure you are not sedentary & are moving as much as possible. Take stairs not the lift!


4. No portion control: Most of us don’t know a dish’s actual serving size, which can lead to overeating even if it is a healthy swap. 


E.g. 1 whole medium avocado contains about 240 calories & 22 g of fat. This will lead to weight gain.


5. Having liquid calories: 1 glass of a healthy smoothie with nuts & seeds can give you 350 to 400 calories. Similarly, a fancy cold coffee can be 450 calories.


Alcohol can also contribute to additional calories!

data.title: White rice makes you fat?
data.description:
White rice makes you fat? 

Here is a detailed explanation for you that should help in regards to all your queries pertaining to white rice.


White rice (polished) especially that is cooked in a rice cooker or a pressure cooker where the excess water (starch) is not removed will over time make you fat. 


This is because it is high in starch which causes a quick spike in your blood sugar. The glucose enters the bloodstream quickly & the excess is stored in the liver which converts to glycogen & if unused gets converted to fat. 


This is how you can include white rice into your diet without fearing weight gain. 


1. Use semi-polished rice & throw the excess water out. This ensures a good amount of starch is reduced.


2. Have this rice with a half-filled plate of veggies in salad or sabzi form & a quarter of the plate with a protein like dal, pulses, or lean meats. This ensures a slower breakdown of starch & keeps you full for a longer period.


The more resistant starch, the fewer calories we can absorb so works well when it comes to weight management. You can reduce the starch content by using rice that is cooked the previous day & refrigerated. Keep it in the refrigerator in an air-tight container for at least 12 to 15 hours.

data.title: Should we add ghee to our diets?
data.description:
Our favourite desi superfood!


Loved by dadis, nanis & mummies :) So ghee or no ghee ??? Should we ? Should we not? How much should we?


My take :


#Ghee is a saturated fat just like what is found in butter, cheese, milk or other milk products. We have Modern day saturated fats we find in biscuits, cakes, puffs, mayonnaise too. Most of them are in INVISIBLE form.


When our older generation spoke of the benefits of ghee, India was a nation that ate roti, subji, dal, and rice predominantly and not Pav bhaji, pizza, nachos or other dishes that came with their amounts of saturated fats.


We ate delicacies only at festivals, laddoos only happened in winter and chikkis in January.. now it’s all available throughout the year.


So it is our choice now, whether to get our saturated fat from ghee by eating shuddh desi food seasonally or create a balance by including modern foods in smart amounts.


We can’t have ghee roti or desi food for lunch AND pizza (cheese)or Paav bhaji (butter) for dinner.


Older generations worked hard, and had to walk miles to get water or wash clothes..we must realize we are 3% as active as them.


So let’s not be under the impression of ghee is good and so is cheese and butter, it’s always a smart choice we need to make

data.title: The Japanese have the lowest rates of obesity & and diseases.
data.description:
The Japanese have the lowest rates of obesity & and diseases.


Here is what we can learn from them :)


1. The traditional Japanese diet is a whole-foods-based diet rich in fish, seaweed and plant-based foods. They do not eat processed foods.


2. traditional Japanese cuisine consists of small portions of simple, fresh, and seasonal ingredients.


The use of chopsticks also reduces large amounts of food ingestion.


Portion control well followed!


3. Hot green tea or cold barley tea are the beverages of choice, while alcoholic drinks like beer and sake are rare. Snacks are uncommon and seldom eaten.

So no snacking on junk!


4. The traditional Japanese diet is naturally rich in various nutrients, including fiber, calcium, potassium, magnesium, iron, and vitamins A, C, and E & low in salts sugar and fat.


Thus we can certainly start following their dietary guidelines:)


This eating pattern is rich in nutrients and may provide numerous health benefits, including improved weight loss, digestion, longevity, and overall health.

👉 A single serving of nuts therefore must not be more than 2 to 3 pieces at a time. 


CONCLUSION:

EXAMPLE: 


Walnuts are in high omega fats, cashews in magnesium & so on. 


So, consuming 2 to 3 each day is enough for fueling your body with nutrients essential for a healthy body and mind.

data.title: Chia Seeds can cause constipation
data.description:
Chia seeds are rich in fiber, protein, omega-3 fatty acids, antioxidants and micronutrients.


They may aid weight loss and help reduce inflammation, blood cholesterol and triglycerides.


However, you have to be careful while adding them to your diet:


👉 Chia seeds are able to absorb 10–12 times their weight in liquid.


👉 If they’re not soaked before you eat them, they may expand and cause a blockage, increasing your risk of choking.


👉 Chia seeds can lower blood sugar and blood pressure. People on medications for high blood pressure or diabetes should moderate their portion sizes to prevent interactions.


Consult a Nutritionist before adding them to the diet.

data.title: Snacking on nuts thinking its healthy??
data.description:
Nuts are healthy, filling & contain very good amounts of essential plant fat as well as fibre...


However, they are also high in calories & lead to weight gain if eaten in high portions regularly.


Here are things to be careful of before adding nuts to your diet:


1. Portion sizes & Frequency: 

You may think that eating five almonds in a day is very healthy. But if you compare and add up your monthly consumption of almonds, it is 150, almonds being consumed in a matter of 30 days! 


2. Having roasted, salted or flavoured nuts. This increases your calories exponentially. So be very careful and have only unsalted nuts.


3. Having nut butters with added sugars, flavours and salts. 


These again come in the weight gain category!


4. Adding nuts to high-calorie dishes like shakes, biryanis and desserts. 


Remember to eat in portions & avoid them if you are obese until you lose some weight :)

data.title: WHITE RICE Vs BROWN RICE 🍚
data.description:
WHITE RICE Vs BROWN RICE 🍚 


Let’s put an end to this shall we?


👉 White Rice 


White Rice Is good Source of Vitamin B1 & B3


Milling & polishing removes the most of the fiber


It is Low in Potassium & Contains 50% less Manganese and Iron


Increases the risk of Type 2 Diabetes & high cholesterol


May cause weight gain


👉 Brown Rice


Excellent source of Vitamin B1 & B3


Good source of dietary fiber & Contains Potassium.


Fat found is heart friendly & is Rich source of Manganese


Reduces risk of Type 2 Diabetes & Controls blood cholesterol 


Helps in weight loss


P.S: I can see a lot of reels getting made saying that brown rice is unhealthy and should not be eaten because of its high content of arsenic. 


However, the fact is that our ancestors always had brown rice which was unpolished. Nobody is asking people to have rice with the husk. So, let us not believe in every thing that is said in a very technical manner, but also use a little bit of common sense. The best brown rice is the one that is partially polished as you can see in the reel. 


It is easier to cook, tastier and also keeps you full for a longer time. That is why it helps in weight loss as well as blood sugar management.

data.title: Can a diabetic have coconut water??
data.description:
Most of us think a diabetic cannot have coconut water & that it will have an impact on their blood sugars. 


However, here are some facts about coconut water:


1. Coconut water has a low glycemic index and contains a very low amount of natural sugar which doesn’t lead to a spike in your glucose levels.


2. With low sugar content, coconut water is suitable for people with diabetes. 


3. Coconut water in fact helps control blood sugar thanks to its high content of potassium, manganese, magnesium, vitamin C, and L - arginine.


IMPORTANT TIP:

 If you are thirsty, in need of a refreshing drink & craving cola or juice, this is a far better alternative. 


Be sure to choose an unsweetened variety and limit your intake to 1–2 cups (240–280 ml) per day.


For uncontrolled & very high blood sugar, add 1 tsp of chia seeds to the coconut water, let them swell up for 30 min, add a pinch of cinnamon powder & have.

data.title: WHY NO POTATOES IN THE DIET 👇
data.description:
WHY NO POTATOES IN THE DIET 👇


Yes, potatoes in principle are not high in calories & if not fried or made into recipes like samosas, tikkis & parathas. 


The reason why we avoid potatoes in diets plans is this:

A potato in India is mostly considered a vegetable (sabzi) and is had with roti, bread, or rice. However, nutritionally, it is not a vegetable but a starch just like roti or rice. It is similar in calories to wheat rice or bread & also high in starch. 


For example, 100g of potato contains almost 100 calories & in the same amount of cabbage, its only 25 calories. So, we ensure clients eat grains and cereals, that too whole grains (brown rice, jowar, multigrain) as they have high fiber, are more filling & low calorie. 


We can’t give you double carbs as that is not the ideal plating for a balanced meal. So while potatoes are not bad, they can be left out while on a diet that is otherwise healthy & filling. 


Going a bit deeper, if you see a traditional American meal, it has meat (steak), vegetables & either potato, bread or corn. This means their starch option is also limited to 1. 


Hope this helped 🙂

data.title: Until necessary, Ketchups are best avoided
data.description:
Side effects of ketchup are often neglected by its users. Tomato ketchup is a sauce used as a condiment. 


The main ingredients of tomato ketchup besides tomatoes are sugar, salt & preservatives.


Home-made ketchups also contain high amounts of salt & sugar making it a very unhealthy addition to our diet.


Let’s see the top side effects of ketchup:


👉🏻 Acid reflux

Tomatoes are also rich in citric and malic acid. When too much of these substances accumulate in your stomach, it will cause heartburn or acid reflux.


👉🏻 Allergic reactions

Consuming food that is rich in this compound leads to the development of rashes. It also causes allergic reactions. 


👉🏻 Joint Pain

It is rich in solanine. This is a compound that builds up calcium in the tissues. Excess amounts thereof cause inflammation and body pains.


👉🏻 Diarrhea

Raw tomatoes are susceptible to salmonella contamination which causes diarrhea.


👉🏻 Kidney Stone

Since the fruit is rich in oxalate and calcium, it is not easily broken down if you consume excessive amounts of these nutrients possibly lead to the formation of kidney stones.

data.title: Palak Paneer: Good to taste, but ZERO in Nutrition!!
data.description:
Palak Paneer: Good to taste, but ZERO in Nutrition!!


Yes, that’s true. This is because ‘Palak’ is a source of iron and ‘Paneer’ is a source of calcium.


When we consume Palak & Paneer together, the absorption of iron in Palak by our body is blocked by calcium in the paneer.


Thus depriving the body of iron which is a very essential mineral that forms blood.


Still, craving for palak paneer????

Many of our future members (i like to call those who inquire with us this) ask me, “Khyati, is your program only diet or is there a compulsory exercise?”


Khyati says, “ I see you are super busy with your day, you hardly seem to have time to commit to any form of physical activity. 


Moreover, if I make it compulsory for you to exercise AND follow a diet, you’ll end up frustrated and hate me and diet & exercise.

You can’t.... Similarly, you can’t OUT EXERCISE a bad meal you had the next day by doing 20 extra minutes.


As for any weight loss/fitness program, food plays an 80% role & exercising can accelerate the process as you burn more calories.


Give 100% efforts to control 1 aspect of weight loss then add another.

data.title: Common mistakes at Iftar that have a negative impact on health & weight loss:
data.description:
❌ Drinking large amounts of water at Iftar time


✔️ IMPACT: Filling the stomach with water is more strenuous to it than with food.


Solution: Can have few sips during Iftar. Later, a glass full of water every two hours.


❌ Drinking Roohafza on daily basis


✔️ IMPACT: It contains high amounts of added sugars, preservatives, and food colors.


Solution: Can be consumed twice a week if required.


❌ Chewing and swallowing food quickly


✔️ IMPACT: Increases unhealthy weight gain and slows down the digestion process.


Solution: Chewing food slowly will speed up digestion and helps in maintaining a healthy weight.


❌ Consuming foods high in sodium


✔️ IMPACT: Sodium triggers thirst through out the fasting hours of the day.


Solution: Consume foods dense in potassium as they retain water and suppress thirst. Banana can be consumed during Suhoor, so as to control thirst levels throughout the day. Other foods such as milk, dates, avocados, pistachios, dried peaches, pumpkin, peas and dark chocolates (in minimum amounts) can be consumed.


❌ Intake of sweets/desserts soon after Iftar


✔️ IMPACT: Leaves you feeling drowsy and sleepy.


Solution: Wait for two hours of having an Iftar meal to stay fresh and awake for Isha Prayers.


❌ Exercising directly after Iftar


✔️ IMPACT: Adverse effects on blood flow as it is concentrated around the stomach during Iftar.


Solution: To ease digestion, work out after two hours of intake.

data.title: Nut Milk VS Regular Milk
data.description:
Nut Milk vs. Regular Milk: Which is Better? 


Many of us are moving to nut milk & oat milk instead of regular animal milk that we have had for centuries. 

Ever wondered about the calorie content & other parameters of your milk choices? 


Let’s break it down & understand better.

 In 100ml of cow’s milk, you’ll find approximately 42 calories, while the same amount of almond or soy milk contains around 40-50 calories. Surprised? Don’t be! 

Most kinds of milk, except full-fat buffalo milk offer similar calorie counts. 

Plus, cow’s milk contains about 3.25g of fat and 12mg of cholesterol per 100ml, more or less similar to nut milk variations.


But here’s the kicker: while nut milk boasts health benefits, be wary of added preservatives like carrageenan, guar gum, and xanthan gum. 

Most nut milks are primarily water with nuts comprising only about 5% of the ingredients. Watch out for those additives. 


P.S. If you are not lactose intolerant & have access to unadulterated fresh milk, pick the low-fat version if this one. 

It has highly bioavailable calcium, class 1 protein & also Fat-soluble vitamins A, D, E & K.

So, if you’re on a weight loss journey, choose either with caution!

data.title: Try these tips to fix your sleep cycle
data.description:
Want to know how to sleep better? 


Look no further than the following tips that will help ensure that you actually have a good night! These tips inevitably come along with many more health benefits.


1. Take the stairs: It enhances heart and lung function and improves blood circulation.


2. Shop for groceries & daily essentials offline: One of the most effective ways to reduce belly fat is to regularly take part in aerobic exercise, such as walking.


3. Be active throughout the day: Walking throughout the day greatly increases cardiovascular fitness, strengthens bones, reduces excess body fat, and boosts muscle power and endurance.


4. Spend an entire day doing some mentally taxing but happy task: Staying mentally active plays a critical role in improving your sleep quality. People who lead intellectually stimulating lives are more likely to be free of dementia conditions like Alzheimer's disease.

data.title: Low calorie Mango Mithai
data.description:
Mango Mithai Recipe for Weight Watchers!


If you are on a path to losing weight, are having PCOS, or have high blood sugars but want to enjoy mangoes, here is how you can :)


INGREDIENTS:

- Oats - 15 g

- Skim Milk / Nut Milk - 75 ml

- Mangoes (chopped) - 1 no.

- Nuts (roasted, crushed) - 3 nos.


DIRECTIONS:

- In a jar or bowl combine all ingredients together. 

- Cover the jar or bowl and place in the fridge overnight or for at least 4 hours. 

- Remove from fridge, and enjoy.


P.S You can have 1 bowl of this thrice a week in breakfast or 2 katoris of this per week as dessert :)

data.title: 5 Spices from your kitchen to help you lose "10 kg in 90 days"
data.description:
These 5 spices will help you lose 10 kg in 90 days!


Weight loss at Balance Nutrition does not need expensive supplements, powders, or foods. 


We take a look at your kitchen ingredients and add them to your diet in the right amounts!


- Kasuri Methi is a rich source of fiber & iron. It can be a great addition to your meals. It not only adds flavor to food but is also a good source of nourishment. People who suffer from diabetes and heart problems should add this to their diet.


- Curry leaves can be beneficial for your skin, hair, and overall health. Rich in vitamin A, vitamin B, vitamin C, vitamin B2, calcium & iron.


- Chewing or eating curry leaves regularly helps in the burning of calories & avoiding fat accumulation. Curry leaves contain Mahanimbine, an alkaloid that has anti-obesity and lipid-lowering effects.


- The curcumin in Haldi suppresses fat tissue growth. It helps in losing weight by regulating sugar levels & preventing insulin resistance.


- Til is Rich in mono-unsaturated fatty acid, which improves the good cholesterol. Lowers blood glucose and improves insulin sensitivity.


- 1/2 tsp Cinnamon powder with 1 glass of water has been shown to have a very positive impact on weight loss, diabetes and overall health.


- Lemongrass tea helps kick-start your metabolism & helps lose weight. This tea helps in detoxification & also boosts metabolism. Replace your soft drinks with lemongrass tea for effective weight loss. It is also low in calories!

data.title: 5 Indian street food that you can have when on a diet!
data.description:
5 Indian street food that you can have when on a diet!


Chana Chaat: a cheap, high protein, low fat snack, that you can get easily in most parts of our country.


Dry Bhel: very low-fat. If you skip the sev and low carb if you replace the potatoes with sprouts or roasted chana. This is also very filling as a big portion of dry bhel has barely any calories.


Moongdal Chilla: The protein content of 1 besan chilla is almost 7.86g, added benefit for vegetarians looking for a protein-rich diet. 


 Idli Sambar: This is the staple breakfast of most Indians across the country. It is rich in probiotics and also protein. Low in calories and if you skip the coconut Chutney, it is low in fat as well.


Mumbai Sandwich: Dirch the white bread and opt for whole wheat or multigrain. Tell him to skip the butter and the potatoes.


*exercise caution & prioritize hygiene before you choose the option*

data.title: 3 healthy alternative to sheer khurma this EID (2024)
data.description:
Eid Mubarak from Balance Nutrition! Wishing you a healthy and nourishing celebration! 💯 


Check out our nutritious recipes for guilt-free indulgence - 

1. Sugar Free Apple Cookies

Ingredients - 

Rolled oats - 1 tbsp 

Unsweetened peanut butter - 1 tbsp

Walnuts (crushed) - 1 tsp

Cinnamon powder - 1 tsp

Apple - 1 medium


Method - 

Combine the oats and cinnamon in a dry skillet over medium heat and toast, stirring occasionally, until the oats are golden. Cool before using.

Core and slice the apple into 6 rounds.

Spread 1 tbsp of peanut butter on 3 of the apple slices and sprinkle with walnuts and oats.

Top with the remaining apple slices to form sandwiches.


2. Split Banana Coco Bites 

Ingredients - 

Banana Medium - 1 no.

Muesli - 1 tbsp

Chocolate Syrup - 1 tbsp

Peanut Butter - 2 tsp


Method - 

Take 1 banana and cut it vertically into 2 halves

Apply 1 tsp of peanut butter on each slice.

Add some muesli and drizzle chocolate syrup on top of it.


3. Fruit Bowl for Pre-Diabetics 

Ingredients - 

Low-fat yogurt - ¾ bowl

Muesli - 2 tsp

Apple - 3 tbsp chopped

Any fruit of your choice - 3 tsp

Walnuts - 1tsp. [crushed]

Raisins - 1 tsp


Method - 

Layer 1/3rd cup of low-fat yogurt into the bottom of a tall glass.

Put a layer of chopped apple, alternately with yogurt and the fruit of your choice until glasses are filled to the top. 

Add muesli on the top layer with crushed walnuts and raisins. 

Serve parfaits immediately to relish the yummy taste.


[balanced eating] [eid mubarak] [nutrition] [festive treats]

data.description:
Facts about Weight Training & Weight Loss-


Most trainers are at war with dietitians as we recommend weight training only after the client loses some weight while they advocate weight training immediately.


Whether you should do weights or not depends on-


1. Goal: If you are looking to lose weight on the weighing scale & want to bring down the weight from 80 to 70 kg, then weight training may not be ideal for you. 


As explained in the Video, you will need to perform LSAT. 


2. Bulk Up: If your goal is to gain muscle mass, not to get weight loss on the scale, then you can do weight training. 


So, be wise & pick your exercise choices 😃


While hitting the weights is undoubtedly beneficial, achieving weight loss goals requires a comprehensive approach. Here's the lowdown:


🍽️ Your Diet Matters: Aim for a diet that's rich in fiber and low in fat. And for even better results, try this plan: Long, Sustainable, Aerobic, and Training.


Remember, sustainable weight loss isn't achieved overnight. It's a journey that requires dedication, consistency, and a holistic approach.

data.title: I started losing weight a lot faster after changing this 1 thing in my diet!
data.description:
While losing 40 kg, I realised that it was never about weighing your food, counting your calories, managing your meals on time or eliminating foods.


What helped me the most was this 1 hack.


It is also backed by a lot of scientific research, and there is also evidence that you can lose weight, especially body fat with this particular change in your day to day diet. 


The star of the show here is fiber.


I am aware of the current protein talk that is making rounds all over Social Media.


While there is no denial in protein and its importance, an overweight person needs to be adding fibre to the diet.


What is fibre? 


Fiber is a type of carbohydrate that the body can’t digest. Fiber cannot be broken down into sugar & passes through the body undigested. 


Fiber regulates the body’s use of sugars, helping to keep hunger & blood sugar & cholesterol in check.


We need 30-35 g of fiber per day, but get only about 15 g! 


Once we fix this, weight loss is bound to happen.


What did I do to get this right?


I simply started all my meals with fibre.


In breakfast, I added oats or apple along with boiled egg whites. No breads.


Lunch & dinner always started with a salad. I included brown rice or millet Roti instead of white rice or wheat phulka. 


P.S - if you are not careful about your water intake, this could cause constipation. So stay hydrated.


(Weight loss) (Fiber intake) (Hydration) (Meal structure) (Carbohydrate) (Protein)

data.title: Lose weight without dieting
data.description:
Join us as @nutritionist_khyatirupani , our head nutritionist, shares her expert insights on achieving a balanced and healthy lifestyle! 💡


1: Incorporate turmeric into your diet-

👉Add half a teaspoon of turmeric powder to your meals. You can also mix it into warm milk or tea or simply in the water. 

👉Turmeric contains a bioactive compound called curcumin, which has powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Adding turmeric to your diet helps in reducing inflammation, supports immune function and improves digestion.

(You can add a pinch of Pepper powder in your meals as it increases the absorption of Curcumin)


2: Enjoy a serving of fruit daily-

👉Make it a habit to include at least one serving of fruit in your daily before your lunch. 

👉Fruits are rich in essential vitamins, minerals, fiber, and antioxidants, which are crucial for maintaining good health. Additionally, it helps to control appetite, keeping you feeling fuller for longer and preventing overeating.


3: Aim for 7,000 steps per day 

👉Incorporate walking into your daily routine by taking short walks during breaks, using stairs instead of elevators, and scheduling longer walks in the morning or evening

👉Walking is a simple yet effective form of exercise that offers numerous health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health and increased calorie burn


Follow us on our social media platforms to get more insightful tips on your journey to a healthier lifestyle! 


(health) (fitness) (fitness goals) (balanced diet) (tips)

data.title: I started losing weight the momemt i stopped eating these healthy foods
data.description:
Eating healthy and eating to lose weight are two different conditions. One has to deal with them properly.


When on a diet to lose weight, there are certain foods that should be eliminated from your meals, even though they are healthy. 


Healthy does not mean low calories or free from fat. 


1 g fat gives us 9 cal. This is more than double the calories from carbs or protein. 1 g carbs and protein have 4 cal. 


A typical weight loss diet has to be low in calories and to ensure that you don’t feel hungry, it has to be high in fibre and protein that is not high in fat.


So, instead of 1 tablespoon butter, which will give you 50 cal, it is better to add one whole apple giving you 90 cal. It is zero fat, high in carbohydrates mainly fiber. This keeps you full for long.


5 HEALTHY FOODS THAT CAN SLOW YOUR WEIGHT LOSS 


✅ Avocado:

While packed with healthy fats, avocados are calorie-dense. 100g contains 200 cal & 19g fat. 


✅ Smoothies: 

These become calorie bombs when loaded with seeds and nut butters & milk. 


✅ Nut Butters: 

Rich in protein and healthy fats, nut butters add up in 600 calories in 100g. 


✅ Diet & Roasted Snacks:

100g of Roasted Banana chips contains 519 calories. Same as fried! 


✅ Sugar-Free Sweets: 

Sugar free is not calorie free or fat free. 100g of sugar free Anjeer Barfi contains 317 cal!


(healthy) (food) (calorie intake) (weight loss) (tips) (advice)

data.title: Khyati Rupani (4 frame transformation)
data.description:
Being a Clinical Nutritionist at Lilavati Hospital, I am not proud to say that I was overweight & developed severe PCOS & went from 68 kg to 99 kg in 1 year. 🛑


I was 25 years old & I was told that to conceive, I had to resort to IVF or IUI treatments & ways. 🚼


That was the time when I decided to find answers in my books & started studying Nutrition in a new light. 📖


I did not give up on gluten, rice, or even dairy while trying to regularise my periods. 

I ate simple, homemade & locally available food. I socialized often so I devised a

BN Restaurant Guide

BN Alcohol Guide 

BN Portion Control Guide 


In 3 months, I lost 12 kg with 99% Diet and 1% Exercise. After reaching 60kg in 6 months, I naturally conceived. Our Diet program Beat PCOS is a mix of all my successful plans, guides & recipes. 

After Trissha was born, my weight again jumped to 98 kg post-delivery due to the history of severe PCOS.

Along with breastfeeding, I started to develop diets for mothers like me & ensured I lost all the weight before Trissha’s first birthday. 

Trissha was breastfed for 16 months & I was back to my pre-pregnancy weight of 60 kg. 

That is when the program Weight Loss Pro, ideal for post-pregnancy weight loss mothers came into being for our clients. 

Foods in this plan included healthy meals & also galactogogues (milk-enhancers).


After Trissha turned 5, I decided to try Intermittent fasting & since then, have been practicing it for the last 8 years. 

I have MAINTAINED the lost weight smartly with the BN Guides that our clients also use in their programs with us. ⚖️

data.title: Me: I'll strictly follow my diet from today. Also Me: 30 mins later..
data.description:
When you were super motivated starting your diet but by the time you get lunch you regret your whole life 😂


 How will the diet be?? Too strict?


My take -

A good dietitian takes into consideration a lot of factors before chalking out a chart. Most important are a client’s ethnic, geographical and lifestyle preferences. 


A client living alone in the USA or the Middle East will not be able to cope with a hot fresh Indian breakfast, or roti sabzi dal lunch.... so it is very necessary to take a look at stores and foods available around to make a sensible and practical chart.


India is a very diverse country and the foods vary from region to religion - a south Indian can’t imagine his diet without rice whereas a north Indian will never be able to follow a soup and salad meal.


We have got the best results in clients where we have followed the above principles ✅

data.title: When I lose 2 kg in a week eating carbs!
data.description:
Not getting enough carbs can be bad. Eating too many carbs is bad. What does a person to do? 🤷🏻‍♀


Many people reduce carbohydrate intake to help them lose weight, which shouldn't be a concern at all!!!!


Carbohydrates are the most efficient fuel for the brain, the internal functions of the body as well as a very healthy source for energy.


Western diets swear by high protein as they always are meat-eaters. High fat in meats has harmful effects on cardiac health.


On the other hand, diets rich in fiber and complex carbs always prove to be safer, healthier, and have high satiety making one feel full for long.


So avoid simple sugars, potatoes, pressure-cooked white rice, refined flour, sago in case you want to lose weight and stick to oats, millets instead.


The right carbs are your friends in weight loss.

data.title: Is Diet coke Healthy? (Tip 13)
data.description:

Bang - theory tells us it causes cancer & is harmful. So we banned it from the diets of our clients.


Like most of you go to Google & see how they can turn a simple cold symptom to pneumonia, we too read up things that were printed in our journals.


When I read about carcinogens recently, I figured that they have categories & aspartame falls in level 2B. It says this could be possibly carcinogenic. This list has 94 other foods which include aloe vera, pickled vegetables & more.


So, having a glass of diet Coke instead of regular Coke is a much better idea to have the diet versions instead.


1 glass diet coke = 0 calories & 0g Sugar

1 glass regular coke = 150 calories & 30g sugar‼️

Benefits -

Flaxseeds: - High in fiber, which facilitates fullness and aids in digestion, hence preventing overeating. - Has omega-3 fatty acids, which may help decrease cholesterol and are good for the heart. -


Cinnamon: - Lowers cravings for sweet meals by assisting in blood sugar regulation. - Has antioxidants, which may lessen inflammation and minimize the chance of developing chronic illnesses.

INGREDIENTS:

👩‍🍳 How to Prepare: -

- For ten to fifteen minutes, add two teaspoons of whole or powdered flaxseed to one cup of hot water.

- After straining the hot liquid, stir in ½ teaspoon each of lemon juice and cinnamon powder.

P.S : To get results, Along with this tea, you must also follow a low fat healthy diet

data.title: My belly fat drastically reduced when I did this one thing
data.description:
While losing 40 kg, I realised that it was never about weighing your food, counting your calories, managing your meals on time or eliminating foods.


What helped me the most to get rid of body fat & belly fat was this 1 hack.


It is also backed by a lot of scientific research, and there is also evidence that you can lose weight, especially belly fat with this particular change in your day to day diet.


I stopped consuming these 3 WHITE FOODS TOTALLY.


1. SUGAR (honey, jaggery, maple syrup as well).

2. ⁠WHITE BREAD (slices, pav,kulcha)

3. ⁠WHITE RICE


I also stopped having any foods that had additional sugar like biscuits, chocolates, India, sweets, and simple ketchup as well.


Fruits became the source of getting my sugar fix and also helped me battle cravings.


Instead of white bread, I started having sourdough, multigrain bread, which also was very rare.


As Indians, we are blessed with plenty of millets for every season. Jawar Roti, ragi Roti, Makai Roti became my best friends.


Lastly, brown rice, and millets like Fox became my staples.


If you have noticed what is common in the above restrictions is refined carbohydrates.


You do not put on weight by adding fats to your diet, but you put on a lot of weight as well as fat around your belly by adding a lot of these refined carbohydrates to your diet.


I simply exchanged them with complex carbohydrates and fiber.

Fiber cannot be broken down into sugar & passes through the body undigested.


Fiber regulates the body’s use of sugars, helping to keep hunger & blood sugar & cholesterol in check.


We need 30-35 g of fiber per day, but get only about 15 g!


Once we fix this, belly fat reduction is bound to happen!


P.S - if you are not careful about your water intake, this could cause constipation. So stay hydrated.


(Weight loss) (Fiber intake) (Hydration) (Meal structure) (Carbohydrate) (Protein)

data.title: When I stopped making these 3 diet mistakes, my weight dropped faster!
data.description:
While losing 40 kg, I realised that it was never about weighing your food, counting your calories, managing your meals on time or eliminating foods.


What helped me the most to get rid of body fat & belly fat was realising these 3 mistakes I was making with my food.


It is also backed by a lot of scientific research, and there is also evidence that you can lose weight with these changes in your day to day diet.


MISTAKE 1:


I was trying to count my calories.

While it is necessary to understand the rough estimate of calories, I did it for every morsel of food. It was getting me frustrated & I started hating food overall.

I went off track many times & cheated.


More than calories, it is important to understand portion size.


MISTAKE 2:


Not estimating liquid calories.


You can add 2000 cal in your daily diet by consuming a milkshake, cold drinks, frappes & fruit juice.


MISTAKE 3:

Confusing thirst with hunger.


Many of us don’t realise that the body is thirsty, not hungry. This is because of ignoring the thirst signals body was sending us. Now, the thirst signal gets impaired. Leads to excessive consumption of food.


If you are hungry, have water first, then go for food.


(counting your calories) (weight loss) (day-to-day diet) (body fat & belly fat) (healthy food) (fruit juice) (hunger)

data.title: Sugar Free Foods
data.description:
Have you stopped sugar completely, taking only ‘sugar free’ foods in your diet?

Despite doing this there is no weight loss?


Here is why 👇🏼


When you see “sugar-free” on a label, it’s tempting to think it’s the healthier weight-loss option. But sugar-free foods have the same, in fact, more calories than

regular versions.


Take Osmania biscuits from @karachibakery as an example:

👉 Regular Osmania biscuits (100g): ~520 calories, 25g fat

👉 Sugar-free Osmania biscuits (100g): ~480 calories, 22g fat


While the sugar-free version has less sugar, the calorie difference isn’t significant enough to justify overeating them.

Plus, sugar substitutes can sometimes trick your brain into craving more, leading to overeating.


Always check the nutrition label! Just because it’s sugar-free doesn’t mean it fits your weight-loss goals. 🧐


(sugar free cookies, Hyderabad, Osmania Biscuits, weight loss)

data.title: Masala Cashew vs Plain Cashew

Nuts, whether raw or masala-coated, are calorie-dense. Here’s why plain & unsalted cashews are a healthier choice:


• Lower Calories: Raw cashews naturally contain fewer calories than masala-fried versions.

• Reduced Fat: Fried cashews come with added oils, raising both fat and calorie content.

• No Added Sodium: Plain cashews are free from the extra salt used in masala seasoning.

• Better Nutrient Retention: Raw cashews retain more nutrients since they aren’t exposed to high-heat frying.


P.S. Limit yourself to 8-10 nuts a day this festive season.

data.title: Winter weight loss super food under Rs.30!
data.description:
Dill is a good source of essential nutrients such as vitamins A and C, calcium, and iron.

These nutrients play crucial roles in boosting immunity, maintaining overall health and can support metabolism increase.


Dietary Fiber Content:

- Dill contains dietary fiber, which aids in digestion and promotes a feeling of fullness. Fiber-rich foods can contribute to satiety, potentially reducing overall calorie intake.


Antioxidant Properties:

- Dill possesses antioxidant compounds, including flavonoids and polyphenols. Antioxidants help combat oxidative stress in the body, which is linked to various health issues. Managing oxidative stress may indirectly support weight management.


Anti-Inflammatory Effects:

- Chronic inflammation is associated with obesity and other health problems. Dill exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, potentially helping to mitigate inflammation and contribute to overall health.


Regulation of Blood Sugar Levels:

- Some studies suggest that dill may help regulate blood sugar levels. Stable blood sugar levels are important for weight management, as fluctuations can lead to increased cravings and overeating.


Digestive Aid:

- Dill has been traditionally used as a digestive aid. It may help alleviate digestive issues such as bloating and indigestion, promoting a healthy gut environment, which is essential for overall well-being.


Potential Diuretic Effects:

- Dill has mild diuretic properties, which means it may increase urine production. While not a primary mechanism for weight loss, it can contribute to temporary water weight reduction.


Supports Hormonal Balance:

- Some compounds in dill have been studied for their potential to influence hormonal balance, by exhibiting estrogenic effects. Hormonal imbalances can contribute to weight gain, and maintaining a healthy hormonal profile is important for overall health.


SO ADD THIS TO YOUR ROTI, SOUPS, RAITAS & JUICES TOO!


DM us the recipes you make at home too :)

data.title: What are the benefits of brushing with neem twigs?
data.description:
Eliminate oral bacteria – While brushing your teeth with neem twigs, you have to chew their tender barks. The antibacterial juice produced in this activity will flow into your mouth. It helps in killing the harmful microorganisms in your mouth.


Natural toothache reliever – Similar to stopping bacteria growth, neem sticks are powerful to soothe the throbbing nerves underneath a tooth. Hence it is considered a natural remedy to treat toothache. If you sense an aching feeling in a tooth, take a need twig. Bite and chew its corner with the problematic tooth. It will help you in overcoming the pain


Strengthen the gums – Researches show that chewing neem sticks can improve the immune response in gums and teeth. It can safeguard the gum tissues hence that the chances of plaque build-up and gum infections can be reduced to a great extent.


Helps in teeth whitening – Besides antibacterial and anti-fungal agents, the active compounds released while chewing neem sticks have whitening properties also. It means that brushing your teeth with neem twigs would remove enamel stains and discolorations.


Apart from neem twigs, chewing neem leaves is also helpful in keeping the alkaline level in your saliva. It inhibits the growth of potentially dangerous oral bacteria.


Will you try this tooth brush? Tell us in comments!

data.title: Do you end up having a lot of snacks while watching TV or Working?
data.description:
Here are a few ways you can avoid mindless eating or snacking -


• Acknowledge the importance of snacks for a healthy meal routine. Don’t deprive yourself of your favourite foods. It will lead to a lot of overthinking & munching.


• Make a proper snack time in your schedule. Ask your dietitian to add meal options.


• Plan healthy snacks during meal prep to avoid overeating and to get in tune with your hunger cues.


• Control your portion and savour every bite. Eat slowly.


• Create a healthy work environment when you are eating, avoid distractions like screen time or engaging in a conversation.


Choose low calorie or zero calorie foods like cucumber, puffed rice, carrot sticks, roasted chana and fresh fruit bites.

data.title: Peanut butter & weight loss
data.description:
Peanut butter is a relatively unprocessed food if made at home. It’s just peanuts, often roasted, ground until they turn into a paste.


This isn’t necessarily true for many commercial brands of peanut butter. These contain various added ingredients, such as:


Sugar

Vegetable oils

Trans fat


And eating too much-added sugar and trans fat has been linked to various health conditions, such as heart disease.


Two tablespoons (32 grams) of natural peanut butter include: Calories: 190.

Protein: 8 grams.

Fat: 16 grams.


This is very high amount of calories and fat, given the small volume of food & may not be considered an ideal option for your weight loss goals.

data.title: Does refrigerated rice have less calories?
data.description:
Refrigerated rice does not contribute to the instant rise in sugar levels, but it contributes less and very slowly to the rise in blood sugar levels due to the presence of resistant starch.


Fresh rice contains more carbohydrates and, hence, more GI and GL value than cooled and reheated rice.


Hence, cooled and reheated rice should be preferred more to fresh rice to prevent the increased spike in the blood glucose levels in the body.


But this doesn’t mean it has reduced calories or will help you lose weight if eaten in huge portions.


This rice needs to be eaten with fresh veggies, dal, chicken & in portions to see weight loss!

data.title: cholesterol, oils, and fats!
data.description:
All you need to know about cholesterol, oils, and fats!


Watch the Video Carefully to remember which oils are to be used according to the dish you make.


Important points to remember:


👉 All oils are cholesterol-free - Cholesterol is a substance that is found only in animals. Your milk and curd will have cholesterol whereas sunflower oil will not because it is from a plant.


👉 Plant sources have other fats - Oils that come from plants have fatty acids and triglycerides. These are a type of fat and very different from cholesterol. So you have to have them sparingly as well.


👉 Cholesterol is saturated fat. So all oils are naturally free from cholesterol.


👉 PUFA & MUFA are fatty acids that are found in oils and other plant sources. These are unsaturated fats.


👉 Trans fats are extremely harmful - These are artificial fats like vanaspati ghee and margarine.

data.title: Brown rice / White rice?
data.description:
The battle between Brown Rice & White Rice Continues to Be Legendary! 🌾

Here are some facts about both of them. 


👉 White Rice:


• While white rice does offer some Vitamin B1 & B3, the milling & polishing process strips away much of its fiber.

• It’s low in potassium & contains significantly less manganese and iron.

• Having polished white rice regularly may elevate the risk of high blood sugars, insulin spikes & weight gain. 


👉 Brown Rice:


- An excellent source of vitamins B1 & B3.

- Packed with dietary fiber & potassium.

- It is heart-friendly & rich in manganese making it a healthy choice.

- Reduces the risk of Type 2 Diabetes & aids in controlling blood cholesterol.

- Promotes weight loss.


P.S.: You may have seen numerous reels claiming that brown rice is unhealthy due to its arsenic content. 

However, it’s essential to note that our ancestors regularly consumed unpolished brown rice without husk. 

The key is to use common sense and opt for partially polished brown rice, as showcased in the reel. 

It’s easier to cook, more flavorful, and keeps you satiated for longer periods, aiding in weight loss and blood sugar management.

Did you know that weight loss is not just about the number of calories you consume in a day?

If you want to lose weight & sustain your diet for the long term, then you have to focus on the foods that are *HIGH in Volume & Low in Calories.*


👍 ✅ Foods that are high Volume & Low Calories:


1. Watermelon (250g): 75 calories

2. Berries (1 cup): 80 calories

3. 1 bowl Clear Veg Soup: 50 calories

4. 1 bowl bhel: 120 calories

5. 1 bowl Saute cabbage: 65 calories


These kinds of foods are very helpful when added to your main meals or snacks as they keep you FULL making it easy to lose weight.

2. Flax/Pumpkin seeds: 80 calories

3. Full Fat Paneer (100g): 265 calories

These foods are not apt for weight loss as they are low in volume & have very many calories.

data.title: Tofu / panner
data.description:
Which One Wins for Weight Loss? **


If you’re condused between Paneer and Tofu to get your protein while on a weight loss diet, let’s break it down!


Both are rich in protein & good for vegetarians, but Tofu edges out when it comes to weight loss. Here’s why:


**Macros:**

- Paneer: A lot Higher in fats and calories. The saturated fats are not ideal for anyone trying to lose weight or battling a bad lipid profile report.

- Tofu: Lower in fats, calories, and packed with plant-based protein. This makes it an ideal choice for vegans too.


**Micros:**

- Tofu is an also powerhouse of iron, calcium, and magnesium, with ZERO cholesterol! 🌱

- Paneer lacks these key minerals and contains saturated fats.


**Weight Loss Win:**


- Tofu’s lower calorie and fat content make it a perfect pick for for overweight individuals while keeping your protein intake high.


(Weightloss protein calories comparison)

- Disease when identified can be cured by Medical interventions, lifestyle changes, Mental outlook towards the disease.
