data.title: Rohan Mirchandani
data.description:
Rohan Mirchandani, co-founder of Epigamia, sadly passed away at just 42 years of age due to a cardiac arrest. 


He was known to be quite active, with an interest in basketball. The company he founded also made Greek yogurt, which is healthy and high-protein.


Heart attacks in people aged 25-44 have surged by 30% in just three years. I personally lost a very close family member to the same cause a few months ago. He too was healthy, ate clean, was slim, and fairly active.


The easiest thing to do is link it to chronic work stress and get away with it.


In my opinion, there are a lot of other factors that could contribute to sudden cardiac arrests, such as:

1. Excessive gymming and physical activity,

2. Sleep deprivation and late-night screen time,

3. Not listening to the body and mind when they are tired,

4. Overfeeding the body with packaged foods (especially those that call themselves healthy and mislead),

5. Ignoring symptoms like gastric troubles, arm and shoulder pain,

6. Overthinking,

7. Over-analyzing,

8. Taking supplements without any prescription.


At times, I always think that life is what we make of it. It is always OUR CHOICE, whether it’s the amount of stress we take or the food and lifestyle choices we make.


I am going to read up and do in-depth research on this for the next few months. Hoping to write something more substantial then.


What do you think is the most overlooked factor contributing to the rise in sudden cardiac arrests among young individuals?

data.title: One Liner
data.description:
YOU DONT WANNA MISS THIS👇🏻


But first, LIKE & FOLLOW for daily fitness tips✨


5 Things to Do After a High-Calorie Meal (Backed by Science & Practical Tips)


1️⃣Go for a Walk (10-15 Minutes Post-Meal)


Studies show that a 10-15 minute walk after eating can lower post-meal blood glucose spikes, especially after a carb-heavy meal.


✅eg. If you had a bug Indian thali or a pizza binge, take a brisk walk around your home or office instead of sitting down immediately. Even light household chores count!


2️⃣Hydrate Well (Flush Out Excess Sodium & Aid Digestion)


High-calorie meals often come with excess salt, fat & refined carbs, which can lead to bloating & water retention. Drinking 500-700mL of water post meal can help restore balance.


3️⃣Make Your Next Meal Lighter (Balance It Out)


 Instead of feeling guilty and skipping meals, focus on protein, fibre and water-rich foods in your next meal to stabilise hunger & metabolism.


✅eg. If you had a ghee masala dosa with coconut chutney, make your next meal lighter with a protein-rich salad or dal soup.


4️⃣Avoid Overcompensating


Many people try to “undo” a high-calorie meal by starving themselves of over-exercising.


✅eg. If you overate at a wedding buffet, don’t skip breakfast the next day. Instead, opt for boiled moong chaat or a simple dal-chawal meal to keep your metabolism steady.


5️⃣Get Enough Sleep


Poor sleep can worsen bloating, increase cravings & impact metabolism. After a high-calorie meal, your body is processing excess energy, & quality sleep helps regulate hunger hormones like lepton & ghrelin.


✅eg. If you’ve indulged in a big dinner, avoid late-night snacking & sleep for atleast 7-8 hours.


One heavy meal doesn’t mean all progress is lost! Instead of guilt-tripping, focus on movement, hydration, mindful eating & consistency.

data.title: Hunger static post
data.description:
The most common reason for WEIGHT GAIN among Indians!


Most of us do not recognize the THIRST signal. Hence, they often eat when they are actually thirsty.


How to train your brain to respond to the THIRST Signal


1. First, drink a glass of water and wait ten minutes before eating again.

2. After 10 minutes, if you’re still hungry, have a cup of black coffee without sugar.

3. Wait for another 10 minutes. If you’re still hungry then It’s time to have a healthy snack.


This simple technique of listening to your body’s messages can make a big difference. Not only will it help you differentiate between thirst and hunger, but it will also help you lose weight by keeping you from the extra food you eat.


Will you follow this hack?


(weight loss, hungry, drink water, healthy lifestyle)

data.title: 5 Things to Do After a HighCalorie Meal
data.description:
YOU DON’T WANNA MISS THIS👇🏻

But first, LIKE & FOLLOW for daily fitness tips✨


5 Things to Do After a High-Calorie Meal (Backed by Science & Practical Tips)


1⃣Go for a Walk (10-15 Minutes Post-Meal)

Studies show that a 10-15 minute walk after eating can lower post-meal blood glucose spikes,

especially after a carb-heavy meal.

✅eg. If you had a big Indian thali or a pizza binge, take a brisk walk around your home or

office instead of sitting down immediately. Even light household chores count!


2⃣Hydrate Well (Flush Out Excess Sodium & Aid Digestion)

High-calorie meals often come with excess salt, fat & refined carbs, which can lead to bloating &

water retention. Drinking 500-700mL of water post meal can help restore balance.


3⃣Make Your Next Meal Lighter (Balance It Out)

Instead of feeling guilty and skipping meals, focus on protein, fibre and water-rich foods in your

next meal to stabilise hunger & metabolism.

✅eg. If you had a ghee masala dosa with coconut chutney, make your next meal lighter with a

protein-rich salad or dal soup.


4⃣Avoid Overcompensating

Many people try to “undo” a high-calorie meal by starving themselves or over-exercising.

✅eg. If you overate at a wedding buffet, don’t skip breakfast the next day. Instead, opt for

boiled moong chaat or a simple dal-chawal meal to keep your metabolism steady.


5⃣Get Enough Sleep

Poor sleep can worsen bloating, increase cravings & impact metabolism. After a high-calorie

meal, your body is processing excess energy, & quality sleep helps regulate hunger hormones

like lepton & ghrelin.

✅eg. If you’ve indulged in a big dinner, avoid late-night snacking & sleep for atleast 7-8 hours.

One heavy meal doesn’t mean all progress is lost! Instead of guilt-tripping, focus on movement,

hydration, mindful eating & consistency.


📌Save this post for the next time you overeat.


(Fitness tips, weight loss, healthy habits, post-binge recovery, mindful eating, calorie balance,

fitness mindset)

data.title: Kapil Sharma sudden weightloss
data.description:
Kapil Sharma’s Weight Loss — Ozempic or Something Else?


As a nutritionist who’s spent 20 years helping people lose weight, reverse PCOS, diabetes, and other health issues… I’ve seen trends come and go.


And right now, everyone’s talking about Kapil Sharma’s sudden weight loss — and whispering: Did he take Ozempic?


I get it — celebrity transformations are fascinating.

But here’s my honest opinion…


Even if he did take Ozempic, should you follow that path? 


YES, you must.. If you want to welcome: 


🔸 Gut issues that linger

🔸 Hormonal imbalances

🔸 Weight regain after stopping


It’s not just about looking thinner. It’s about feeling well, sleeping better, having stable energy, and being in charge of your health, without injections or side effects.


So what’s the best way to lose weight or manage health concerns?


✅ Eat food that nourishes you, not punishes you

✅ Balance your hormones with authentic meals, not meds

data.title: How to eat pav baji without guilt?
data.description:
As a nutritionist, I get asked this all the time: “Do I have to give up my favorite Indian street foods to eat healthy?”

And my answer? Absolutely NOT.


Pav Bhaji is a gorgeous blend of vegetables, spices, and pure comfort.


Here’s my step-by-step hack to enjoy your favorite bhaji without the guilt:


1️⃣ Start with a warm, clear vegetable soup. Don’t touch the bhaji until it’s finished!

2️⃣ Have half of your green salad before diving into the bhaji! 

3️⃣ Top your bhaji with chaas instead of buttered pavs. You’ll eat less and feel fuller.

4️⃣ When you’re ready, try ordering your pav without butter. Trust me, small changes make a BIG difference!


You’re not just cutting calories — you’re nourishing your body, boosting digestion, and still enjoying every bite. Step by step, healthier habits win!

data.title: Tahira Kashyap fights with cancer
data.description:
When life challenges you again & again, you either break or rise. Tahira Kashyap? She rises with grace, grit, and grounded wisdom. 


As a nutritionist, I see her battle with cancer not just as one of resilience but as a living testament to the powerful link between self-awareness, early detection, and whole-body wellness.


Cancer isn’t just a physical battle it’s a lifestyle wake-up call. 


Tahira’s courage to share her journey publicly, as she enters Round 2, is not only brave, it’s transformative. 

She’s reframing illness as a chance to reconnect with our bodies, habits, and health. And one thing she drives home? Early detection is everything. 


Regular mammograms aren’t an option; they’re an act of self-love. 


Her metaphor is perfect: “When life throws lemons... add kala khatta and sip it with good intentions.” 


As someone who works closely with individuals on healing from the inside out, I’ve seen how whole-food nutrition, anti-inflammatory habits, and emotional wellbeing all support recovery not just from disease, but from stress, fatigue, and the silent wear-and-tear we often ignore.


Tahira’s story isn’t just inspiring, it’s instructional. It’s a call for all of us to listen more closely to our bodies, to choose food that fuels us, habits that heal us, and screenings that could save us.


So let this be a moment not just of admiration, but of action. Start with your plate. Start with your calendar. Start with yourself.

Early Puberty Alert: Key Insights from Khyati Rupani


Did you know that girls as young as 8 years old are experiencing early puberty? Khyati Rupani from Balance Nutrition shared some eye-opening insights today about the **causal factors** behind this alarming trend.


🔹 Sedentary Lifestyle: Less physical activity means more health risks. 

🔹 Ultra-Processed Foods: High in sugars and unhealthy fats, these foods are wreaking havoc on young bodies.

🔹 Stress: Increased stress levels can also contribute to the early onset of puberty. 

🔹 Obesity: Higher body mass index (BMI) can trigger early puberty.

🔹Environmental Factors: Exposure to certain chemicals and pollutants can disrupt hormonal balance.

🔹Genetic Factors: Certain genetic mutations can lead to early puberty.


It’s time to take action and make healthier choices for our children’s future! 


Full Video Link in bio!

data.title: Strawbeery reel
data.description:
Strawberries: The Desi Blueberries! 🍓 


Welcome to the One-Minute Wonder Series with Khyati Rupani! 


Did you know that strawberries are as powerful as blueberries when it comes to antioxidants, vitamins, and weight loss? 


1. Rich in Antioxidants:

Strawberries are loaded with vitamin C and flavonoids that fight free radicals, boosting skin health and immunity.


2. Low in Calories:

With only about 32 calories per 100 grams, they’re perfect for weight loss and calorie-conscious diets.


3. High Fiber Content:

They aid digestion, prevent constipation, and keep you feeling full longer, supporting gut health.


4. Heart-Healthy:

Strawberries are known to reduce bad cholesterol levels and lower blood pressure, thanks to their potassium and antioxidant content.


5. Regulates Blood Sugar:

The fiber and polyphenols in strawberries help stabilize blood sugar levels, making them a diabetic-friendly fruit.

6. Boosts Skin Glow:

The vitamin C in strawberries promotes collagen production, improving skin elasticity and reducing wrinkles.

7. Rich in Essential Nutrients:

They are a great source of manganese, folate, and potassium, which are crucial for bone health and brain function.


Which is your favourite strawberry recipe? 

Tell us in comments & we’ll tell you a healthier version of it 🤩


(Strawberry, blueberry, antioxidant, weightloss)

100g of Amla = 600-700mg of Vitamin C – That’s 10x more than an orange!


Since Vitamin C is water-soluble, we usually lose it during cooking.

But why doesn’t cooking destroy Amla’s Vitamin C?

data.title: OZEMPIC
data.description:
The Link Between Ozempic, Mounjaro & Thyroid Cancer Risk: What We Need to Know


New research has linked popular weight-loss injections like Ozempic, Wegovy, and Mounjaro to a higher risk of thyroid cancer.


A study of 350,000+ patients found that the likelihood of thyroid cancer was significantly higher within the first year of use.


📌 Key Findings:

✔️ Research on rodents previously suggested a link between semaglutide (Ozempic, Wegovy) and medullary thyroid cancer (MTC).

✔️ This new study, tracking patients for six years, found a higher incidence of thyroid cancer.


⚠️ My Concerns as a Nutritionist:


1. I have had clients who developed thyroid cancer while using these injections.

2. Female clients have experienced an increase in fibroids.

3. A link between these injections and hysterectomy has also been observed.

4. High usage of these drugs is seen in places where insurance covers them, implying economic benefits for many.


Would I recommend these injections for diabetes or weight loss?

NO.


Whether it is diabetes or obesity, simple changes in your diet and lifestyle are key to controlling—and even reversing—diabetes.


Tried and tested drugs should be used with caution under the supervision of expert doctors for uncontrolled diabetes.

data.title: Rose Day
data.description:
Comment ‘Rose’ if you need help in losing weight


Feeling bloated or uncomfortable? Try this soothing Rose Petal Fennel Detox Tea for instant relief!


Rose Petal Fennel Detox Tea

(Instant bloating relief, gut-friendly, anti-inflammatory)


Ingredients:

1 tbsp dried rose petals

1 tsp fennel seeds (saunf)

½ tsp carom seeds (ajwain)

1-inch piece fresh ginger (grated)

1.5 cups water


How to Make It:

1. Bring 1.5 cups of water to a boil in a pan.

2. Add fennel seeds, carom seeds, and grated ginger. Simmer on low heat.

3. Turn off the heat and add dried rose petals. Cover and let it steep for 5 minutes.

4. Strain and serve.


How It Works:

Rose petals soothe digestion, reduce inflammation, and relax stomach muscles, while fennel seeds effectively combat gas and bloating.


Ideal Time to Drink This Tea:

Post-meal (30–45 minutes after eating): Aids digestion, prevents bloating, and reduces inflammation.

For chronic bloating/inflammation: Best consumed in the morning on an empty stomach.

At night: Helps relax digestion and reduce nighttime bloating.


How Many Days Should You Have It?

For long-term anti-inflammatory benefits: Drink 5 days a week for a month, then as needed.


(Roseday, Health, nutrition, valentineweek, Healthylife)

data.title: Chocolate day reel
data.description:
Comment ‘YES’ if you’re ready to start your fat-loss journey the right way!


When I lost 40 kg, I realized that weight loss was never about obsessively weighing food, counting every calorie, or eliminating entire food groups. Instead, what truly helped me shed body fat—especially belly fat—was identifying and fixing these three key mistakes in my diet.


Backed by scientific research, these small but impactful changes can help you lose weight without frustration!


MISTAKE 1: Obsessing Over Calories

I used to count every single calorie, which left me frustrated and made me hate food. I went off track multiple times and ended up bingeing.


Instead of calorie counting, focus on portion control—it’s far more sustainable!


MISTAKE 2: Ignoring Liquid Calories

Drinks like milkshakes, sodas, frappes, and even fruit juices can unknowingly add up to 2,000 extra calories a day! Cutting down on these made a massive difference in my fat loss.


MISTAKE 3 (The Biggest One!): Over-Restricting My Favorite Foods

I used to have chocolate after every meal but decided to eliminate it completely. That backfired! Instead, I made smarter swaps:


✅ Switched from high-sugar milk chocolate to dark chocolate

✅ Ate half a piece instead of two

✅ Limited it to two meals per week instead of daily

✅ Reduced intake gradually, not abruptly


Sustainable weight loss isn’t about extreme restriction—it’s about balance!


(Chocolate Day, Balance diet, Portion control, Weightloss)

data.title: Inflammation Reel
data.description:
Could you have inflammation?


Then you’ll want to read this.


After nearly seven months of research, my R&D team and I are thrilled to introduce ADVANCED versions of two of our most successful online diet programs.


This journey began when I turned 40 last year and suddenly noticed changes in my mood, bloating, periods, and even body fat.


With my adenomyosis (a type of endometriosis), things only got worse. That’s when my team and I at Balance Nutrition decided to deep dive into the impact of inflammation—especially in women and men over 30.


We studied how gluten, dairy, stress, and packaged foods affect overall well-being, especially for those experiencing inflammation due to an underlying health issue.


Although my diet programs have always been naturally anti-inflammatory, we have now developed ‘Intermittent Fasting Advanced’ & ‘Plateau Breaker Advanced’—specifically designed to address the root cause of inflammation.


Key Features

✔ Anti-inflammatory diets to target inflammation

✔ Available only in 90-day & 180-day formats

✔ A focused approach to reducing inflammation

✔ Incorporates anti-inflammatory foods you already have in your kitchen

data.title: Meri Saheli Dark Sugar reel
data.description:
YOU DON’T WANNA MISS THIS👇


But first, LIKE and FOLLOW for more daily fitness tips like this!


The Truth About ‘Sugar-Free’ Products! 


In my recent podcast with Meri Saheli, we busted one of the biggest myths about sugar-free products. Many people assume sugar-free means healthy, but that’s far from the truth!


🔸 Most sugar-free alternatives have more fat and calories than regular products.

🔸 For diabetics, the real problem isn’t just sugar—it’s the entire processed package.

🔸 The “sugar-free” label tricks us into eating more. Instead of one regular biscuit, you might eat four sugar-free ones!

🔸 Sugar-free sweets made with figs and dates still spike blood sugar—just because they don’t contain white sugar doesn’t mean they’re safe.

data.title: The Brisk Podcast (hereditary)
data.description:
Just because your parents have diabetes doesn’t mean you will! 


 Here’s the REAL reason you might get it…👇”


Genetics alone does NOT cause diabetes—it’s your lifestyle choices that trigger it. 


❌ Frequent blood sugar spikes – Too much sugar & refined carbs lead to insulin resistance.

❌ Being sedentary – Lack of movement reduces your body’s ability to regulate blood sugar.

❌ Chronic stress & poor sleep – High cortisol messes with insulin function.

❌ Belly fat accumulation – Visceral fat is directly linked to metabolic disorders.

❌ Nutrient deficiencies – Low fiber, magnesium & omega-3 levels worsen insulin resistance.


Tag a friend who needs to know this in the comments & spread awareness.


(Diabetes, Hereditary, Lifestyle, Nutrition, Healthy Diet)

data.title: IWD COOKING OIL
data.description:
Think "Cold-Pressed" Means Fewer Calories? Think Again! 🛑


Most people think some oils are "healthier" than others because they're cold-pressed or labeled as heart-friendly. But here’s the truth that Nutritionist Khyati Rupani decodes in our latest podcast: 🎉🤯✨


🔥 All oils are 100% cholesterol-free (because only animal products contain cholesterol).

🔥 Every oil has the SAME calories – whether it's olive, coconut, or vegetable oil.

🔥 What matters most is the type of fat inside – some oils are packed with healthy fats, others… not so much!


Want to know which oils to prioritize and which to limit? 😫🌟

data.description:
Black Coffee vs. Shroom Coffee – Which One Should You Choose?


@rangeeliruchi @radionasha caught up with Khyati to discuss the real differences between these two trending brews.


☕ Black Coffee:


Zero calories, making it a great choice for those on a calorie deficit

Boosts metabolism, helping burn more calories throughout the day

Acts as an appetite suppressant, supporting weight management

Provides a quick energy boost, enhancing workout performance

🍄 Shroom Coffee:


Lower caffeine content, making it gentler on the gut

Packed with adaptogens that help manage stress and support overall well-being

A great alternative for those sensitive to caffeine, but may not be as effective for metabolic boost and fat burn

data.title: Detox Carousel
data.description:
Do detox diets really work?

Yes!


How?


🌱 Gives Your Digestive System a Break: Allows your gut to reset and recover from heavy or processed foods.

💧 Flushes Out Toxins: Helps eliminate waste and reduces bloating by promoting better hydration.

🌿 Boosts Energy Levels: Light, nutrient-dense foods can improve mental clarity and reduce sluggishness.

🍋 Supports Liver Health: Ingredients like lemon, ginger, and leafy greens enhance liver detoxification.

🥒 Reduces Inflammation: Anti-inflammatory foods and drinks can calm the gut and reduce overall body inflammation.


MOST IMPORTANT:

They give you the confidence to commit to a longer diet or fitness program to help you get healthy.


(detox, weightloss, 1-day diet, cleanse)

data.title: Tip 1 of GUT RESET CHALLENGE
data.description:
BN Gut Reset Challenge Tip 1:


One of the best ways to improve your gut health is to start by treating inflammation and healing the Gut. This Super Gut Drink does exactly this!


Easy Recipe for Morning Lemon and Ginger Water:


Ingredients:


1 glass (about 250 ml) water

1/2 lemon, freshly squeezed

1/2 inch piece of fresh ginger, grated or thinly sliced

2 sticks cinnamon 

A pinch of haldi (turmeric)


Instructions:


Boil water and let it cool slightly until it’s warm but not scalding with ginger & cinnamon sticks.

Switch off the gas & Squeeze the juice of half a lemon into the water.

Add the haldi to it. 

Stir well and let it steep for 2–3 minutes.

Strain if desired, or drink it with the ginger pieces for added benefits.

Enjoy this drink on an empty stomach each morning for optimal results.


Tips for Best Results:

Drink it warm, not hot, to maximize absorption.

Wait 20 minutes before breakfast to allow the ingredients to work on your system.

This practice can set the tone for the rest of the day, supporting digestion, reducing inflammation, and promoting overall gut health—all of which contribute to sustainable weight loss.


Do this for at least 2 weeks to see maximum results!

data.title: 3 indian sweets for diabetics
data.description:
This is how Diabetics can enjoy their favorite sweets during Diwali & manage their blood sugar levels!


1. Rasgulla

• Why It’s Safer: Rasgulla is made from chenna (paneer), giving it a protein base that slows down sugar absorption. Compared to richer milk-based desserts, it has a lighter sugar profile.

• Sugar Spike & Levels: Its sugar syrup can contribute to blood sugar spikes, so squeeze it to reduce residual sugar.

• Portion Size: Stick to one piece, ideally with 4 unsalted nuts (almonds or walnuts) to balance blood sugar response.

• Ideal Way to Eat: Enjoy it after a protein-rich meal to further lessen its impact on blood sugar.


2. Shrikhand (Low Sugar)

• Why It’s Safer: Made with hung curd, shrikhand has protein and probiotics, supporting gut health and slowing down sugar absorption.

• Sugar Spike & Levels: Choose homemade or low-sugar varieties, or add chopped fruits for natural sweetness.

• Portion Size: Aim for 2 tbsp with your meal.

• Ideal Way to Eat: Start with a cup of soaked chia seeds water for fiber that helps manage blood glucose response.


3. Apple Fruit Custard

• Why It’s Safer: A fiber-rich fruit and low-sugar custard help with slower glucose release.

• Sugar Spike & Levels: The fiber delays sugar absorption, and custard can be sweetened with stevia for a lower glycemic load.

• Portion Size: Limit to half a bowl, topped with a pinch of cinnamon to help manage blood sugar.

• Ideal Way to Eat: Best consumed between main meals to prevent overwhelming blood sugar levels.


P.S. If you have uncontrolled diabetes, please consult a nutritionist before adding these to your diet.


(Diabetic-friendly sweets, Blood sugar management, Festive treats, Healthy dessert options)

data.title: 3 foods to avoid
data.description:
3 Foods to Avoid Every Festive Season ✨


1. Soan Papdi

• Loaded with Hydrogenated Fat: High in trans fats, which raise “bad” cholesterol (LDL) and increase heart disease risk.

• Questionable Production Quality: Often made in unsanitary conditions, increasing the risk of contamination.

• Excess Sugar & Calories: Causes quick energy spikes and crashes, making it harder to control cravings.


2. Artificially Flavored & Colored Mithai

• High in Artificial Colors & Additives: Colors like Red #40 and Yellow #5 may trigger digestive issues and allergic reactions.

• Refined Sugars & Chemical Additives: Put extra strain on the liver, making it harder for your body to detoxify and stay balanced.

• Minimal Nutritional Value: These desserts often lack fiber or protein, leaving you feeling unsatisfied.


3. Coke, Pepsi & Other Aerated Drinks

• Empty Calories & High Sugar Content: With zero nutrients, these drinks are just sugar and calories, promoting weight gain and fat accumulation.

• Triggers Cravings: The sugar rush spikes dopamine, increasing cravings for more sweets and junk foods.

• Impacts Bone Health: Phosphoric acid in Coke can interfere with calcium absorption, weakening bones over time.


Wishing you all a healthy & prosperous Diwali! 🎉


( Festive season, Diwali, Healthy eating, Nutritional value, Food choices )

data.title: 5 veg desi food for protein
data.description:
Top 5 Veg Desi Foods to get your daily protein & calcium requirements. 


Calcium is a key building block for bones. But as we age, our bones lose calcium faster, making them fragile. Keeping up with calcium-rich foods can help reduce this loss, protecting against fractures and osteoporosis.


Protein isn’t just for muscle—it’s critical for bone density too! It helps maintain bone mass and strengthens muscle tissues around the bones, which adds support and stability, reducing the risk of falls and fractures.


🌿 Top 5 Desi Veg Sources of Protein & Calcium:


Ragi (Finger Millet) – This has more calcium than milk. Ragi upma with sprouts as breakfast is the best way to add this to your diet. 


Chickpeas & Lentils – Loaded with protein to support bone structure.

Chole, Rajma, Dals & da chillas should be in added to 2 meals in a day. 


Paneer (Indian Cottage Cheese) – Offers both protein and calcium. Use the low fat & home made version to avoid excess calories. 


Sesame Seeds (Til) – High in calcium and help reduce inflammation.

Add this to your roti, paratha or in your Tadka 


Soybeans – Full of protein, they’re a great meat-free source. Please try having tofu bhurji at least once every alternate day to your meals. 


(Osteoarthritis, osteoporosis, high protein diet, high calcium diet, vegetarian protein)

data.title: Karva chauth static post (post)
data.description:
5 diet TIPS for a perfect Karva Chauth Vrat!


Pre Dawn [Sargi]

1. Because you will be fasting, load your nutrients smartly for the day!🤩


2. Eat your sargi in the following order:

- 1 Cup of coconut water to replenish and store your electrolyte levels, essential for fasting

- Hydrating fruits like watermelon, orange, apple which are rich in fiber, water and postassium for energy

- 1 source of fats from Nuts and seeds [2 walnuts/3 to 4 almonds]

- A protein source

- Last is your carbohydrate to midfully load for the fasting!


3. Following a sequence of nutrients will help in managing blood glucose and energy levels till you break the fast without much muscle loss


4. Enjoy your fasting hours with faith in your heart and a smile on your face😄


5. When you break the fast, follow a smart sequence and be gentle to your tummy!


6. Break your fast with a simple glass of coconut water to keep your headache away!


- Replenish your glucose with an easy-to-digest carbohydrate like a date!


- Make sure your meal is easy to digest yet adequate in fiber and proteins so that you don’t shoot your blood glucose as this stores more fats.


- For the sake of your stomach and digestion, NEVER HOG ON LARGE AMOUNTS OF FOOD AFTER A FAST


- Be gentle to your body after it is in a state of fast to allow it to take a limited nutrient load.


Hope you have a happy and healthy Karva Chauth! ✨🎑🌕


( Karva Chauth, Fasting tips, Sargi, Nutrition)

data.title: After 40
data.description:
3 Non-Diet Hacks or Tips That Helped Me Lose Stubborn Body Fat After 40 are as follows:


💥 After losing 40 kg 15 years ago and transforming my life, I realized I was gaining body fat after turning 40 last year. 

I realized my old dietary habits had nothing to do with this gain & there were other factors that I needed to work on.


1️⃣ Tracking my emotions & staying calm 🧘‍♀️ 

Stress plays a huge role in weight gain, especially due to cortisol spikes. I began tracking my emotions daily, learning not to react aggressively and focusing on calmness. It helped me break the stress-fat cycle. 


2️⃣ No starvation, no alcohol 🚫 

Extreme dieting? Not for me. I limited alcohol to 1 glass a fortnight and stopped starving myself unknowingly when at work. My metabolism thanked me for it. 

Nourishing my body with the right foods kept my energy up and helped me lose extra fat sustainably.


3️⃣ Weight training (even when I hated it!) 🏋️‍♀️ 

I’ll admit, I wasn’t a fan of weight training, but doing it just twice a week has been a game changer. It boosted my metabolism, strengthened my muscles, and helped me burn more fat.

data.title: 10,000 steps (calorie burn)
data.description:
“How Many Calories Do You Burn While Walking 10k Steps Each Day?”:


10K step is a viral fitness & weight loss topic worldwide ✅


Walking is a simple yet powerful way to boost your metabolism, improve heart health, and help with weight loss. 

The calories burned can vary depending on factors like gender, weight, and walking speed. 


Here’s a closer look:


✨ For a Person with Normal Weight (Approx. 60-70 kg)

Men: 

On average, a man weighing around 70 kg can burn approximately 400-500 calories. This varies depending on speed and terrain, but it’s a great daily calorie burn.

Women: 

A woman weighing around 60 kg might burn around 300-400 calories with 10k steps.


✨ For an Overweight Individual (Approx. 85-100 kg)


Men: 

If you’re around 90-100 kg, walking 10k steps can burn anywhere from 500-700 calories. The heavier you are, the more calories you burn with each step due to the extra energy required to move your body.


Women: 

For women in the 85-90 kg range, you can expect to burn around 400-600 calories. Walking is a low-impact exercise, making it perfect for those looking to ease into fitness while still burning fat.


💡 What Affects Calorie Burn?


Speed: The faster you walk, the more calories you burn.

Incline: Walking uphill or on rough terrain burns more calories than walking on flat surfaces.

Age: Younger individuals tend to burn calories more efficiently than older adults, though walking remains an effective exercise at any age!


 Do you track your daily steps? Let me know in the comments below!👇

data.title: High Fiber Diets
data.description:
BEST WAY TO LOSE WEIGHT I FOLLOW AS A NUTRITIONIST: A HIGH-FIBRE & LOW FAT DIET. 


Most weight loss diets at Balance Nutrition are high on fiber as that is the best way to keep our clients full & cleanse the gut. 


However, it can sometimes cause a few unwanted side effects like gas, bloating, & stomach discomfort. Here’s why:


1️⃣ Fermentation in the Gut: High-fiber foods are fermented by gut bacteria, leading to gas production.

2️⃣ Sudden Increase: Adding too much fiber too quickly can overwhelm your digestive system.

3️⃣ Slower Digestion: Fiber slows digestion, which can lead to a feeling of fullness and bloating.


🍃 How to Add Fibre & Avoid these Symptoms:


 1️⃣ Increase Gradually: Add fiber-rich foods slowly to your diet, giving your body time to adjust.

2️⃣ Check Water Intake: Drink plenty of water to help fiber move smoothly through your digestive system.

3️⃣ Move More: Gentle exercise like walking can reduce bloating and help ease digestion.

data.title: Tip 1 of SLIM SMART with BN
data.description:
Tip 1 of SLIM SMART (Weight & Hormone Balancing Challenge) with BN


Many of us believe that eating healthy meals is the key to lose weight. While it is important to eat healthy, we must not forget the most important component leading to weight loss.


That is knowing your portion size. 


Like it is explained in the video, most of us are misled by a lot of food companies on the nutritive value and especially the portion sizes.


You will see various advertisements where the portions are shown different, and the ideal portion that you are supposed to be eating is completely different.


If we take the comparison of the muesli in the bowl and that in the jar, there is a difference of 250 cal that you can add extra if you do not follow portion size.


If you are hungry and need food beyond the recommended portion for you, always resort to high-volume low calorie foods. We will describe those in detail in the reels coming up.


So if your weight is stuck, it may not your hormones or your age, just simple mistakes like ideal portion knowledge.

data.title: Tip 2 of SLIM SMART with BN
data.description:
TIP 2 of the Slim Smart Challenge!


I am sure a lot of you will relate to this tip. 


Calories can also come in the form of invisible calories. 

These are cals that come from hidden oil, fat, hidden sugar, and you are not aware that you are having such high calorie foods. 


A lot of us don’t even consider the oil used in cooking as a source of calories. 


A typical India Dal or sabji can get very high in caloric due to the oil added in tadka. 


1tsp oil = 45 cals 


If you add around 10 teaspoon oil, you are adding 450 cal to your green sabzi or healthy home made yellow dal. 


That too just oil calories these are. Additional ones from other main ingredients is yet to be added.


So, if your weight is stuck, and if you are trying to eat home-cooked meals, which are healthy, don’t forget to consider your Tadka calories :) 


A lot of foods that we also order are not exactly healthy. Stirfry veggies can accommodate 7 teaspoon oil per serving 😄

data.title: Tip 7 of SLIM SMART with BN
data.description:
“Roasted Snacks is a huge business in the world of weight loss. As a nutritionist, for me there is no gimmick bigger than this when it comes to actual results 


Here is why :) 


1. Calorie Content:


 • Roasted Chips: Although seemingly healthier, a large serving can still pack in a lot of calories.

 • Fried Chips: Higher in calories per gram compared to roasted, but in moderation, they can be part of a balanced diet.


 2. Fat Content:


 • Roasted Chips: Often contain added oils and fats to achieve that crispy texture.

 • Fried Chips: Typically higher in fat, but enjoying them occasionally in small portions is less harmful than overeating roasted chips.


 3. Portion Control:


 • It’s easier to overeat roasted chips due to the perception that they are healthier, leading to higher calorie and fat intake.


 4. Moderation is Key:


 • Enjoying fried chips in small amounts can be better for your diet than consuming large portions of roasted chips, which can still be calorie-dense.


So, follow the 2-bite rule or Focus on portion sizes and balance in your diet. 


It’s not just about the type of food but how much and how often you consume it. 


Stay healthy and mindful, everyone! 🌟 


(2 bite rule) (roasted snacks) (portion control) (weight loss) (nutritionist)

data.title: Winter Juice
data.description:
INTERMITTENT FASTING - IS IT THE BEST?


For most of you, intermittent fasting is all about ensuring that you are fasting for a long period of time. The whole stress is given to the fasting hours. 


While there is a lot of substantial evidence about the benefits of fasting, it only works if you’re eating window is appropriately managed with proper eating pattern and diet. You cannot call it intermittent fasting if you’re eating window comprises of all the junk and wrong foods. 


In short, intermittent fasting does not work with Only fasting hours. 

It works with a balance of fasting and a good diet plan during the eating window. 


To know more, DM us :)

data.title: Sambhar Rice
data.description:
Lose weight with Rice in your diet :)


Time & again, rice has been villainized to be the culprit when it comes to weight gain.


Rice is not at all an issue. A serving of boiled rice contains 100 calories. 

So does wheat, ragi & other millets. 


What’s the difference? It is the fiber content & therefore the GI (glycemic index)


White polished rice is low in fiber, and high in starch & will therefore have a high GI making it unhealthy for overweight individuals & diabetics. 


How to ensure you are eating Rice the right way?


1. Use Brown Rice 

2. Use Unpolished Rice

3. Use Basmati Rice & eat after 12 to 20 hours of refrigeration. 

4. add 50% veggies & 20% protein to 1 bowl, the rest 30% can be rice.

data.title: Truth about healthy food
data.description:
The truth about Healthy Food!


Most of us will always go for foods that are labeled - Low-Fat, Vegan & Most favorite Sugar-Free.

Most of these fancy labels do not specify the absolute truth. The amount of calories in these healthy foods at times is higher than in regular foods. 

1. Some smoothies can be a lot higher in calories than coke. 

2. Some sugar-free sweets can be extremely high in fat. 

3. A lot of gluten-free biscuits & bread have double the calories than regular bread. 


We see a lot of clients before starting their program with us very troubled & telling us that they are eating very healthy but still not losing weight. 

This is the main reason. Since it comes with the label ‘Healthy’, the portions sixes are also not kept in control. 

This leads to gain in weight rather than loss. 


If we compare the image above, you can easily see how a healthy smoothie, a salad & a parfait are higher in calories than regular junk. 


To lose weight, it is necessary to understand these labels & also compare the calories with it’s original counterparts. 


Next time you see a sugar free ladoo, be careful to not miss the ghee, the nuts & high calorie dried fruits like dates & Anjeer ;)

data.title: Reverse thyroid reel
data.description:
While losing weight & reversing Thyroid, Our client Shruti realised that it was never about weighing food, counting calories, managing meals on time or eliminating foods.


What helped her the most to get rid of body fat & Thyroid was realising these 3 mistakes she was making with her food. 


MISTAKE 1: 


Counting Calories: 


She was getting frustrated & started hating dieting overall.

She also went off track many times & cheated. 


More than calories, it is important to understand “portion size”.


MISTAKE 2:


Not estimating liquid calories:


You can add 2000 cal in your daily diet by consuming a milkshake, cold drinks, frappes & fruit juice.


MISTAKE 3: 


Confusing thirst with hunger:


Many of us don’t realise that the body is thirsty, not hungry. This is because of ignoring the thirst signal, it gets impaired. Leads to excessive consumption of food. 


If you are hungry, have water first, then go for food.


Along with this, she was on a healthy diet & her mentor was always available to solve her doubts!


(Weight loss) (Thyroid reversal) (Healthy diet) (Portion control) (Liquid calories) (Nutrition mentor) (Healthy habits) (Calorie counting mistakes) (Diet mistakes)

data.title: Sweets or fried food craving
data.description:
While losing 40 kg, I realized it was never about weighing your food, counting your calories or feeling intense cravings.


What helped me the most to get rid of body fat & belly fat was realizing a big mistake I made with my food Cravings. 


THE MISTAKE:


‘I would control my cravings for a very long time, and there would come a point where I would just give in. There are days where I have eaten and entire tub of ice cream or a big piece of cake. I have also done the same mistake with craving of pizza And Samosa.


HOW TO MANAGE CRAVING? 


When ever I now have a grieving for food that is high in calories and not healthy. This is what I and the clients at Balance Nutrition do. 


1. Take two bite-size portions of the food you are craving.

2. ⁠ Filll a glass of water & keep it beside you.

3. ⁠ Eat the first bite.

4. ⁠ Have the glass of water that is around you.

5. ⁠ Wait for a minute and have the second bite.


You will be surprised that you’re craving is satisfied, and this also tricks your body into feeling full after eating your craving.


P.S. follow this tip for anyone food per week only.


(Cravings, hunger, 2 bite rule, weight loss)

data.title: Paneer Tiika Reel
data.description:
While losing 40 kg, I realized it was never about weighing your food, counting your calories, managing your meals on time, or eliminating foods.


What helped me the most to get rid of body fat & belly fat was realizing a big mistake I made with my food. 


It is backed by a lot of scientific research, and there is also evidence that you can lose weight with these changes in your daily diet.


THE MISTAKE:


‘I was adding paneer to my diet daily & paneer tikka was my go-to at every restaurant’!


Now you will think paneer is high in protein & most recommended as a weight loss food.. here are some facts:


1. 100g raw paneer contains: 25g fat & 300 calories 

2. 1 portion paneer tikka: 900 calories 

3. 1 portion paneer bhurji: 450 calories 


Most paneer used in restaurant dishes is full fat; the ones available in the market are also high-fat.


If your goal is to lose weight, then the addition of healthy foods that are high in calories or fat needs to be kept in check. A low-calorie & a low-fat diet with adequate fiber, protein & healthy carbs will help you get here faster.


I added these low calorie vegetarian protein sources to my diet instead and lost weight faster.


Swap for Low-Calorie, High-Protein Alternatives:


Tofu: 76 kcal (100g, 8g protein)

Edamame: 121 kcal (100g, 11g protein)

Lentils: 116 kcal (100g, 9g protein)

Don’t let your diet be derailed by hidden calories!

data.title: All about Volume of Foods & Calories
data.description:
Did you know that weight loss is not just about the number of calories you consume in a day?


Yes,


If you want to lose weight & sustain your diet for the long term, then you have to focus on the foods that are *HIGH in Volume & Low in Calories.*


👍 ✅ Foods that are high Volume & Low Calories:


1. Watermelon (250g): 75 calories

2. Berries (1 cup): 80 calories

3. 1 bowl Clear Veg Soup: 50 calories

4. 1 bowl bhel: 120 calories

5. 1 bowl Saute cabbage: 65 calories


These kinds of foods are very helpful when added to your main meals or snacks as they keep you FULL making it easy to lose weight.


👎🛑 Foods that are Low Volume High Calories:


1. Coconut Chutney (2 tbsp): 105 calories

2. Flax/Pumpkin seeds: 80 calories

3. Full Fat Paneer (100g): 265 calories

4. Nuts (100g): 650 calories

5. 2 Slices Butter Toast: 500 calories


These foods are not apt for weight loss as they are low in volume & have very many calories.


(Weight loss, calories, fat loss) Have you tried eating high-volume & low-calorie foods?

data.description:
*All about Volume of Foods & Calories*


Did you know that weight loss is not just about the number of calories you consume in a day?


Yes,


If you want to lose weight & sustain your diet for the long term, then you have to focus on the foods that are *HIGH in Volume & Low in Calories.*


👍 ✅ Foods that are high Volume & Low Calories: 


1. Watermelon (250g): 75 calories

2. Berries (1 cup): 80 calories 

3. 1 bowl Clear Veg Soup: 50 calories 

4. 1 bowl bhel: 120 calories

These kinds of foods are very helpful when added to your main meals or snacks as they keep you FULL making it easy to lose weight. 


👎🛑 Foods that are Low Volume High Calories:

data.title: DID you know- Body holding fat
data.description:
While losing 40 kg, I realised that it was never about weighing your food, counting your calories, managing your meals on time or eliminating foods.


What helped me the most to get rid of body fat & belly fat was realising these 3 mistakes I was making with my food. 


It is also backed by a lot of scientific research, and there is also evidence that you can lose weight with these changes in your day to day diet.


MISTAKE 1: 


I was trying to count my calories. 

While it is necessary to understand the rough estimate of calories, I did it for every morsel of food. It was getting me frustrated & I started hating food overall.

I went off track many times & cheated. 


More than calories, it is important to understand portion size.


MISTAKE 2:


Not estimating liquid calories.


You can add 2000 cal in your daily diet by consuming a milkshake, so called healthy smoothies & protein shakes, cold drinks, frappes, alcohol or fruit juices!


MISTAKE 3: 

Confusing thirst with hunger.


Many of us don’t realise that the body is thirsty, not hungry. This is because of ignoring the thirst signals body was sending us. 

Now, the thirst signal gets impaired. Leads to excessive consumption of food. 


If you are hungry, have 400ml water first, then opt for food if still hungry after that. 


Are you making any of these mistakes?

data.title: Why Weight Training Causes Weight Gain?
data.description:
After seeing reels on importance of weight lifting at 40, I started it too. 


I was shocked to see a weight gain as my goal was to lose weight. 


Why Weight Training Causes Weight Gain? 


1. **Muscle Mass Increase**: Muscle tissue is denser & weighs more than fat, so as you gain muscle, your weight might increase even if you're losing fat. 


2. **Improved Muscle Glycogen Storage**: 

Glycogen binds with water, leading to a temporary increase in weight due to water retention.


3. **Enhanced Protein Synthesis**: 

After strength training, your body enters a state of increased protein synthesis, which repairs and grows muscle fibers. This process contributes to muscle hypertrophy (growth).

 

 

Whats the best way then to lose weight? 


- **Weight Training**: Focuses on building muscle and strength. It's excellent for body composition changes, increasing muscle mass, and boosting metabolism.

 

- **Cardio**: Effective for burning calories and fat. It improves cardiovascular health and helps create a calorie deficit, essential for weight and fat loss.


### Key Takeaway:


**If you want to lose weight, start with cardio exercises to burn calories and shed fat. Once you achieve your desired weight loss, incorporate strength training to build and maintain muscle mass. This approach ensures long-term results and a toned physique.**


(Weight loss, strength training, muscle loss, protein intake)

data.title: Is peanut butter healthy ?
data.description:
Peanut butter & weight loss:


Peanut butter is a relatively unprocessed food if made at home. It’s basically just peanuts, often roasted, ground until they turn into a paste.


This isn’t necessarily true for many commercial brands of peanut butter. These contain various added ingredients, such as:


sugar

vegetable oils

trans fat


And eating too much added sugar and trans fat has been linked to various health conditions, such as heart disease.


Two tablespoons (32 grams) of natural peanut butter includes: Calories: 190.

Protein: 8 grams.

Fat: 16 grams.


This is very high amount of calories and fat given the small volume of food & may not be considered as an ideal option for your weight loss goals.


(weight loss) (unprocessed food) (trans fat) (sugar) (calories)

data.title: Slim Smart Tip 8: Is Oral Collagen Really Effective?(reel video)
data.description:
Slim Smart Tip 8: 

💡 Is Oral Collagen Really Effective? 💡


Today, I’m debunking a popular myth about oral collagen supplements. Unfortunately, it is of little benefit to you. Here’s the science behind why they might not be as beneficial as you think:


Digestion Breaks It Down: Oral collagen is broken down into amino acids during digestion, losing its original structure and benefits.

Bioavailability Issues: Not all consumed collagen is absorbed; much of it is excreted without being utilized by the body.

Non-Specific Use: The amino acids from collagen are used by the body wherever needed, not necessarily for skin or joint health.

Lack of Evidence: Scientific studies show mixed results on the effectiveness of oral collagen for improving skin elasticity or reducing joint pain.

How can you naturally boost your collagen production? Here’s how:


Vitamin C: Essential for collagen synthesis. 

Protein-Rich Foods: Amino acids from protein sources like meat, fish, beans, and legumes help form collagen.

Copper & Zinc: These minerals are vital for collagen production. Could you include nuts, seeds, and shellfish in your diet?

Healthy Lifestyle: Regular exercise, adequate sleep, avoiding excessive sun exposure, and smoking support collagen health.

data.title: Dadi's Gym
data.description:
While losing 40 kg, I realised that it was never about weighing your food, counting your calories, managing your meals on time or eliminating foods.


What helped me the most was this 1 hack.


It is also backed by a lot of scientific research, and there is also evidence that you can lose weight, especially body fat with this particular change in your day to day diet.


The star of the show here is FLaxseed & Cinnamon Tea!💫


With the help of this revitalizing Flaxseed and Cinnamon tea, transform your health! 🌿 ✨ 


Benefits - 

Flaxseeds: - High in fiber, which facilitates fullness and aids in digestion, hence preventing overeating. - Has omega-3 fatty acids, which may help decrease cholesterol and are good for the heart. - 


Cinnamon: - Lowers cravings for sweet meals by assisting in blood sugar regulation. - Has antioxidants, which may lessen inflammation and minimize the chance of developing chronic illnesses. 


Lime: Packed with vitamin C, this fruit supports healthy skin and strengthens the immune system. - Enhances flavor without raising calorie or sugar content.


INGREDIENTS:

👉 Flaxseeds [whole or ground] - 2 tsp 

👉 Cinnamon powder - ¼ tsp 

👉 Lemon juice - ½ tsp 

👉 Water [boiling water] - 1 cup


👩‍🍳 How to Prepare: - 

- For ten to fifteen minutes, add two teaspoons of whole or powdered flaxseed to one cup of hot water. 

- After straining the hot liquid, stir in ½ teaspoon each of lemon juice and cinnamon powder.

P.S : To get results, Along with this tea, you must also follow a low fat healthy diet


(Balance Nutrition) (Khyati Rupani) (Health tips of the day) (Healthy tea) (Herbal tea)

data.title: Does Fat First work for all?
data.description:
Refrigerated rice does not contribute to the instant rise in sugar levels, but it contributes less and very slowly to the rise in blood sugar levels due to the presence of resistant starch. 


Fresh rice contains more carbohydrates and, hence, more GI and GL value than the cooled and reheated rice.


Hence, cooled and reheated rice should be preferred more to fresh rice to prevent the increased spike in the blood glucose levels in the body.


But this doesn’t mean it has reduced calories or will help you lose weight if eaten in huge portions. 


This rice needs to be eaten with fresh veggies, dal or chicken & in portions to see weight loss!

data.title: 5 Vegan Indian dinners you didn't know of
data.description:
If you are under the impression that going vegan is about eating tofu, cashew cheese, or almond milk, then you are mistaken.


By definition, a vegan diet is best explained this way.


This diet comprises of foods that are plant-based. It means, that, you cannot have anything coming directly or indirectly from animals.


This means,

Alcohol is vegan 🍷

Potato chips fried in plant oil are vegan 🥔

French fries, most breads and biscuits also are vegan 😵


If you ask me about Indian vegan diets, we have been eating vegan food more than anybody else.


Dal rice

Roti sabzi

Rajma rice

Idli sambar

Chole

Dhokla

Chillas

data.title: Comment on - Intermittent fasting causes heart attack!
data.description:
Intermittent fasting causes heart attack! 


This article has caused a huge stir in the world of nutrition and dietetics.


If you all have seen the movie Sanju, Bollywood actor Sanjay Dutt’s biopic… you may recall how newspaper articles put a ?? against every article about him. As per the movie, they did so because that article had no clear evidence. 


Likewise, this article also has a huge MAY BE right in the headline itself.


As a nutrition expert mainly focused on studying & working with the Indian & Arab populations & race, this article does not hold true in many places:


1. It was done by the American Heart Association & included only U.S adult citizens, there is no report on their race, age, other health parameters, or risks they carried. 

2. It was carried out loosely & dependent on the participant’s memory to report food intake & other details. 

3. Major factors like food they ate in the eating window were not considered or studied. 

4. Their health or medical parameters were not studied at all.


Here is what my experience as a practicing Nutritionist for 19 years has taught me:


1. Indians respond to Intermittent Fasting very well due to our traditional eating habits. Indians always ate with the sun & did not have any food after sunset. This was due to religious reasons & also the fact that there was no electricity. The food could be contaminated. 


2. In winter, the fasting hours would naturally go up to 14 - 16 hours as the sun set early. This is when we ate high-calorie ladoos, pinnis, Mathris, Undhiya, makai & sarson to get our calorie needs met. 


**In conclusion, I strongly believe Intermittent fasting, if done under a qualified Nutrition Expert works very well for Indians.


The key factor here will be - the diet followed in the eating window & the hydration maintenance during the fasting window.**

data.title: Do you watch Insta reels before sleeping most nights?
data.description:
Watching reels at bedtime can have a lot of side effects.


En listing a few of them that are related to your overall health and weight. 


1. it disrupts your sleep cycle leading to incomplete rest, which has a lot of physical as well as psychological health hazards.

2. ⁠ Most of you will also want to munch on something while doing so, meaning that you will end up adding more calories late at night.

3. ⁠ This also eats into a lot of our productive time for the next day which is spent battling less sleep.


This also impacts your hormonal balance which leads to a lot of weight gain. 


So, if you want to continue watching reels, ensure that it is only for a stipulated period of time during the day. 


You will be surprised at the amount of free hours you have during your day to accomplish things like cooking, healthy and exercising.

data.title: Mother's day- Being a working women makes...
data.description:
The conflict between leaving work after child birth or leaving your child under somebody else’s care while you are at work can be extremely hard.

Due to PCOS & infertility, I was told that I will never be able to conceive.


While I was able to conceive Trissha naturally due to weight loss & diet, I became an obsessive mother for 6 months of our life.


Since Trissha was a precious pregnancy, I was not able to trust anybody to take care of her.


Needless to say, this was driving me crazy & not allowing me to heal postpartum. 

I was 99kg after delivery, but was not bothered about myself. 


when Trissha was 5 months old, mom started noticing the severity of this. 


Vishal & mom talked me to start consulting again. 


I started to meet clients only for 2 hours a day.


 I was calmer & decided to start consulting mornings too.


Before Trissha, I worked 16 hrs as the chief nutritionist at Lilavati Hospital


I started realising that motherhood was no longer obsessive or stressful.


I could also see Trissha bonding with her grandparents & realised that I could do with the help as well


The most rewarding part about going to work was the welcoming smile & the warm warm hug I got every time I got back home


By the time, Trissha was 2, Balance Nutrition started picking up as well.


I started spending almost 8 hrs at work comfortably. 


I believe, to be a good mother, you have to 1st be good to yourself & ensure your mental health is at an optimal level.


For me, being able to consult clients is the call to my mental well-being & happiness. It also gives me a sense of calm & achievement.


 Trissha has learnt to be independent, can make basic decisions on her own, is a lot more confident. 


Had I been that obsessive mother, I would have not had a wonderful relationship with her today. 


We enjoy our drives post dinner, swims & also arguing about food, time management, studies & lot more 😄


The best part is… We look forward to catching up to argue regularly 😆


Stop obsessing about being the perfect mother, just be happy.


HAPPY MOTHERS DAY ❤️

data.title: Heatwave (loose motion, random acidity, bloat)
data.description:
If your stomach’s throwing a full-on summer tantrum… don’t worry, it’s probably not just you being dramatic, blame the heat! 😵‍💫🔥


Loose motions? Random acidity? That mysterious afternoon bloat?


Nope, it’s not Mercury retrograde. It’s that sketchy leftover you thought was still good. 👀

☀ In this kinda heat, food turns faster than your mood on a Monday morning.


That paneer tikka sitting out “just for a bit”? Yeah... it’s plotting against you.


And when your gut’s upset, your vibes follow. Every. Single. Time.


✨ So, what can you actually do?


🧊 Refrigerate like you mean it, don’t just shove stuff in the fridge, make sure it’s cool enough to keep it fresh.


🥣 Stick to fresh food if it smells “kind of okay,” it’s probably not.


💧 Chaas + a pinch of pink salt = summer lifesaver, hello hydration and happy gut!


🧠 Eat right, feel right, your nutrition is working overtime behind the scenes, so show it some love.


Basically, your gut is texting you in all caps. Time to listen and give it the summer TLC it deserves. 😮‍💨


#HotGirlSummer but for your gut ✨

data.title: Ever noticed how roti, rice, and dal just don’t feel the same anymore? (priya Sachdeva podcast)
data.description:
Ever noticed how roti, rice, and dal just don’t feel the same anymore?


Growing up, these were the staples in our home.

But back then, roti was made from grains ground at home.

Rice wasn’t ready in 10 minutes. It took time, and it had all the fiber.


Now?

It’s all refined.

Roti feels like maida.

Rice is just starch.

We eat, we feel sleepy.

And slowly, our health pays the price. Weight gain, fatigue, even fatty liver.


The truth is, our ancestors knew what they were doing.

They ate whole foods. They took time to cook.

And they didn’t need diets, detoxes, or supplements.


Maybe we don’t need to eat less.

Maybe we just need to eat like they did.

.

data.title: Karan Johar sudden weight loss
data.description:
Karan Johar claims that what helped him lose weight was not Ozempic but OMAD!


 What is OMAD?


OMAD stands for One Meal A Day. You fast for 23 hours and eat just one meal in a one-hour window.


Does it help with weight loss?

 Yes, but only if you eat fewer calories overall.

 Your insulin drops during fasting, pushing the body to burn fat.

 It can backfire if your one meal is ultra-processed or super high-calorie. 


Can Karan Johar’s transformation be just OMAD?


UNLIKELY 


That level of fat loss + facial change + speed point to:

Ozempic or GLP-1s

Fat Loss Supplements

Structured workouts

Medical supervision

OMAD may have helped, but it was likely the whole story.


Should you consider OMAD to lose weight?


For most people, not really.

Side Effects Include - Hunger, mood swings, energy crashes & depression. 

Women, especially due to hormones, struggle more to sustain this diet. 


Was it Ozempic or OMAD


(Karan Johar, Bollywood, Ozempic Debate, Weight Loss, wellness Tips, Healthy Lifestyle)

data.title: Dr. Parvati Madhu lost 15 kg in 90 days with our Intermittent Fasting Program. She followed the 14:10 window. This mom of two from the USA is seeing a positive difference in pre-diabetes, Anemia, and PCOS.
data.description:
Want her diet plan so you can follow it too?


You Should NOT Follow Someone Else’s Diet Chart! 🚫🍽️

Here is why:


1️⃣ Wrong Caloric Intake – What works for them may lead to weight gain or loss for you.

2️⃣ Nutritional Deficiencies – You may miss out on essential vitamins and minerals your body needs.

3️⃣ Metabolic Mismatch – Every person’s metabolism, activity level, and digestion differ.

4️⃣ Worsening of Health Issues – A plan not suited for your medical conditions (PCOS, diabetes, thyroid, etc.) can make things worse.

5️⃣ Unsustainable & Restrictive – You might struggle to stick to it, leading to frustration and binge eating.


✅ Benefits of a Personalised Diet Plan:

✔️ Tailored to Your Body Type – Focuses on YOUR metabolism and goals.

✔️ Supports Medical Conditions – Designed to manage issues like insulin resistance, gut health, and inflammation.

✔️ Includes Foods You Love – Sustainable, enjoyable, and easy to follow.

✔️ Long-Term Results – No quick fixes, only sustainable weight loss and health benefits!

data.title: Should Men And Women Have Different Diets?
data.description:
Should women eat like men? Absolutely not.


Periods, PMS, pregnancy, postpartum, menopause—our hormones fluctuate every single day.

That’s why one-size-fits-all diets don’t work—especially for women.


@nutritionist_khyatirupani from @balancenutrition.in breaks it down beautifully:

✔️ The science behind hormonal cravings vs. emotional cravings

✔️ Why dairy, sugar, and even “healthy” foods may react differently in a woman’s body

✔️ How to eat smarter during different stages of life—without guilt


Whether you’re dealing with bloating, cravings, fatigue, or weight gain after 35, this podcast is your sign to stop ignoring your hormones.

data.title: Are Kids Really Picky Eaters — Or Are We Raising Them That Way?
data.description:
Are Kids Really Picky Eaters — Or Are We Raising Them That Way?


@nutritionist_khyatirupani believes that most kids aren’t born picky — they learn food habits from us.


If a parent avoids vegetables or says “I don’t like this,” kids quickly follow.

And if kids get exposed to sugar, fast food, and packaged snacks early on, their taste buds get used to junk — making dal, roti, sabzi feel “boring.”


The good news? You can shape their food preferences early.

✅ Mix vegetables into parathas or idlis

✅ Add spinach or beetroot to dal or soup

✅ Plate food with colour and creativity


You don’t need imported snacks or nutrition powders.

Just smart Indian food, everyday habits, and a little patience.


📩 Comment “KID FOOD” to get the full podcast link in your DMs!


For more such practical parenting and food tips, follow @podtalkwithekta @ektakhosla23

data.title: This or That
data.description:
Losing weight or improving your health doesn’t mean cutting out everything you enjoy.


As someone who has lost 40kg and reversed PCOS naturally, our founder, Clinical Nutritionist Khyati Rupani, knows firsthand how impractical it can be to give up sweets entirely.


At Balance Nutrition, we use the 2-Bite Rule — a mindful and scientific way to manage cravings for desserts or calorie-dense treats without derailing your progress.


Two mindful bites are often all it takes to curb the craving. No need to rely on extremes or eliminate your favourite foods — we focus on balance, not restriction.


This is how we help clients achieve consistent results in our weight loss, hormonal balance, and gut health programs.


Pro Tip: The rule works best when used in moderation. A few thoughtful bites each month can satisfy the mind — and keep the body on track.


(weight loss India, PCOS weight loss, Indian weight loss program, healthy Indian eating habits, mindful eating, real weight loss journey, fat loss without gym, sugar cravings, craving management, sustainable fat loss, emotional eating, hormonal weight gain, gut healing foods, balanced Indian diet, clinical diet plan, sugar craving fix, no fad diets)

data.title: Best Food for Kids
data.description:
Your Child Doesn’t Need Overcomplicated Food!


As a parent, you don’t need imported superfoods to raise healthy kids.

@nutritionist_khyatirupani from @balancenutrition.in shares everyday, desi staples that support your child’s growth, gut, and brain.


- Buttermilk – hydration + probiotics + protein

- Local green veggies – think drumsticks, cabbage, not kale

- Fresh fruit – mango, watermelon, targola (no milkshakes!)

- Millets – try ragi paratha or jowar roti

- Pulses & dals – the OG plant-based protein


Skip the sugar-filled snacks and start with these simple, locally sourced foods.

data.title: Gut Friendly Diet For Indians
data.description:
Bloating, Fatigue, Dull Skin? It all starts with your gut.


As a nutritionist, I see this all the time clients coming in with bloating, fatigue, or low energy.


Problem?

Unhappy gut.


So, this is an Indian gut-friendly meal plan:


- Ragi & oats - full of fibre, easy on the gut.

- Curd & kadhi - our desi fermentation.

- Papaya, beetroot, dudhi - hydrating, light, and soothing

- Elaichi, warm water, green tea - best for post-meals.


No exotic superfoods.

No complicated routines.


Only simple choices that help your gut (and you) feel a little more balanced.


(Khyati Rupani, Balance Nutrition, Diet Plan, Constipation, Bloating, Acidity)

data.title: Being a Clinical Nutritionist at Lilavati Hospital, I am not proud to say that I was overweight & developed severe PCOS & went from 68 kg to 99 kg in 1 year. 🛑
data.description:
I was 25 years old & I was told that to conceive, I had to resort to IVF or IUI treatments & ways. 🚼


That was the time when I decided to find answers in my books & started studying Nutrition in a new light. 📖


I did not give up on gluten, rice, or even dairy while trying to regularise my periods. 

I ate simple, homemade & locally available food. I socialized often so I devised a

BN Restaurant Guide

BN Alcohol Guide 

BN Portion Control Guide 


In 3 months, I lost 12 kg with 99% Diet and 1% Exercise. After reaching 60kg in 6 months, I naturally conceived. Our Diet program Beat PCOS is a mix of all my successful plans, guides & recipes. 

After Trissha was born, my weight again jumped to 98 kg post-delivery due to the history of severe PCOS.

Along with breastfeeding, I started to develop diets for mothers like me & ensured I lost all the weight before Trissha’s first birthday. 

Trissha was breastfed for 16 months & I was back to my pre-pregnancy weight of 60 kg. 

That is when the program Weight Loss Pro, ideal for post-pregnancy weight loss mothers came into being for our clients. 

Foods in this plan included healthy meals & also galactogogues (milk-enhancers).


After Trissha turned 5, I decided to try Intermittent fasting & since then, have been practicing it for the last 8 years. 

I have MAINTAINED the lost weight smartly with the BN Guides that our clients also use in their programs with us. ⚖️

data.title: Want to know an easy hack for weight loss? (Portion size with palm)
data.description:
Want to know an easy hack for weight loss?


Just use your palm!


It is a common misunderstanding that the only key to weight loss is to eat foods that are healthy and low calorie.


This is where most people go wrong‼️


Well, the truth is that it's not just about eating healthy or low-calorie foods. There is another mantra that is important for weight loss and that is ‘PORTION SIZE’. 


Increased portion sizes are thought to contribute excessive calories and unwanted weight gain. Even healthy binging is harmful to health!


The more you have on your plate, the more you eat & the more you gain!


👉🏻So here’s how you can use just your palm to understand portion size 


1 serving of carbohydrates (cereals, millets)= 1 cupped hand


1 serving of veggies & salads = 1 fist


1 serving of fats (butter, ghee)= 1 thumb 


1 serving of high protein foods (paneer, chicken, fish, tofu) = 1 palm


You need to be wise about your portion size! 


That is why at Balance Nutrition we educate our clients about portion size with our BN exclusive Eat-In- Portions Guide which gives them a clear idea about how much to eat!


To understand well about portion size & get a personalised meal plan, comment 'I want a diet' or

data.title: 4 Reasons why should not add curry leaves to your diet!!
data.description:
If you don’t want to see these changes in your health, exclude curry leaves from your diet!!


1. Curry leaves can be beneficial for your skin, hair, and overall health because it contains phenolic antioxidants 


2. They are Rich in vitamin A, vitamin B, vitamin C, vitamin B2, calcium, iron, potassium, and magnesium.


3. Chewing or eating curry leaves regularly helps in the burning of calories & avoiding fat accumulation.


4. Curry leaves contain Mahanimbine and koenigine, which are potent antioxidants. These compounds have anti-obesity, anti-diabetic, and lipid-lowering effects.


Curry leaves are used in almost every Indian household for garnishing but we often remove it from the dishes without being fully aware of their health benefits! 


P.S.: To all those who are confused, Curry leaves are actually healthy! Just wanted you all to read the caption : )

data.title: Things I no longer do as a nutritionist
data.description:
As a Certified Nutritionist, my journey has been about finding true health through genuine choices. Here are a few things I've left behind, not just professionally but personally too:


🚫Ketchup: Ketchup is one condiment that seems to enter our food unknowingly. I have seen children having all their meals only with ketchup. It is high in sugars, preservatives and should never be used. The healthier options include chutneys made out of tamarind and dates. You will get the same taste.


🚫Sugar-Free sweeteners: Sugar free Indian sweets are made using dates and anjeer both of which are very high in natural sugars as well as calories. Artificial sweeteners when added to a dessert does not make it healthy or calorie free. If you crave a dessert, you must try the two bite rule.


🚫Eat “healthy” biscuits: Even the so called 'healthy' biscuits that claim to be sugar free or low fat, often have a surprising amount of additives, hidden sugars and empty calories.


🚫Suppressing my cravings: Human nature is such that the more you are restricted and refuse to a particular food, the more you will crave it. It is better to smartly add these foods to your diet in good proportion.


🚫Soup Diets: Not just soup diet but any other diets that claim to give good results very quickly are off my list. Weight loss happens only when you sustainably and practically make changes to your diet.


If you want to know how to lose weight in a healthy manner, without having to follow any fad diets or give up on your favourite foods, type "I want a diet" in the comments or DM us!

data.title: You Don't Have To Give Up On Chaat While Dieting
data.description:
Try this cucumber sev puri! It is filled with protein, antioxidants and vitamins and fulfills your cravings without adding extra calories!


Replacing normal fried puri, sev and potato makes it a perfect low calorie evening party snack!


Recipe


Dried white peas - 10 g

Turmeric Powder - 1 pinch

Asafoetida - 1 pinch

Salt - to taste

Oil - ½ tsp

Green chutney - as required.

Sweet date and tamarind chutney - as required.

Cucumber [cut in round slices] - 1 no

Onion, finely chopped - ½ no.

Tomato, finely chopped - ½ no.

Chaat masala - ½ tsp

Lemon juice - 1 tsp

Fresh coriander [for garnish]

Baked sev [for garnish].


Step 1 :

Soaked dried peas in 1 cup water for about 6 hours.


Step 2 :

Drain and boil in 1 & ½ cup water with turmeric, asafoetida and salt till soft.


Step 3 :

Let the ragda cool down.


Step 4 :

Plate cucumber slices, add ragda, finely chopped onions, chaat masala, green chutney, tamarind, sweet dates chutney and squeezed lemon juice like a sev puri.


Step 5 :

Garnish with baked sev and coriander.


So next time you have a guest at your house or you feel like eating chaat, do serve this healthy sev puri and enjoy your snack!

👉🏻Dietary fiber is an important nutrient that aids in digestion. Soaked chia seeds contains 40% fiber which brings them to the top of the list of fiber-rich food!


👉🏻The gel formed is due to mucilage and digestible fiber and helps in stool movements!


👉🏻It not only helps to relieve constipation but also in regulating body weight, blood sugar level & health of the colon!


But don’t forget to drink water throughout the whole day for this recipe to work well or it can cause you issues like bloating, diarrhea & stomach ache


Here’s how you can make it 👇

Recipe:


*Milk of your choice- 100 ml

*Soaked Chia seeds - 1.5 tsp

*Mixed Fruits (avoid mango if diabetic) - 1 katori

*Almonds/Walnuts- 2-3 pieces

*Cinnamon Powder - a pinch


Step 1:

Mix milk and soaked Chia seeds in a bowl


Step 2 :

Store it in the refrigerator for an hour


Step 3 :

Top it with cinnamon, fresh fruit & nuts of your choice!


Try this recipe and let us know if you find any improvements in your health!

data.title: Fat Burning Bullet Coffee
data.description:
This coffee became popular among those following Keto & Low-Carb diets. So adding this to your diet occasionally has quite a few benefits:

P.S. Honey is optional & this coffee is more effective without honey.


1. It is high in MCT, a fat found in butter & coconut oil that directly enters the bloodstream without having to metabolize via your liver.


2. It helps you stay fuller keeping hunger & cravings in control


3. You can use it as a meal replacement instead of breakfast to induce weight loss once a week.


4. It is an ideal pre-workout drink as it leads to an increase in stamina & helps you burn more calories.


5. It also provides a small amount of vitamins like A, D & E.


In conclusion, this drink can be occasionally added to the diet of someone who wants to start weight loss, works out regularly & has no underlying disorder like Diabetes or High cholesterol.

data.title: Low Hemoglobin Beetroot & Carrot Recipe
data.description:

This juice is best to have on an empty stomach daily.


RECIPE:

- 2 Beetroot & Carrot (peeled)

- 2 Amlas

- 1 inch piece of Ginger (Adrak)

- 1/2 Bunch of Spinach (Palak)

- 1 Lime (squeeze the juice)

Blend these ingredients properly in a juicer until puréed. Strain if too thick. Squeeze lime juice to this. Also, you can additionally add a pinch of cinnamon powder to this.


The anti-oxidants & vitamins in this juice will help you cleanse your liver & supply your body with a lot of vitamins!


The addition of lime juice makes the iron from the vegetables easily available for absorption


P.S. Do not throw the residue, use those veggies to make a tikki, chilla or just take it & apply it to your face :)

data.title: Fix Your Gastrointestinal Disorders in One Drink
data.description:
A lot of us are suffering from gastrointestinal disorders.


The common symptoms of this seasonal illness are loose motion, vomiting & diarrhoea.


The immediate danger to our health is that of dehydration. Since the appetite is low, an ORS (Oral rehydration solution) is the 1st line of treatment.


An oral rehydration solution is used to treat moderate dehydration.


It’s made of water, glucose, sodium, and potassium.


BENEFITS:

The combination optimizes the absorption of fluid in the intestines, which helps quickly replenish fluids.


Do like & share!

data.title: Coriander cardamom tea
data.description:
Cleanse your Kidney & Liver with this drink ✅


Coriander and cardamom tea:

🌿 Reduces water retention 

🌿 Lowers blood sugar 

🌿 Lowers blood pressure 

🌿 Helps with kidney detox 

🌿 Helps fight acidity


When to drink?


Have 1 cup before bed for best results


How to prepare?


Required ingredients: 

* Cardamom pieces - 4-5 no. 

* Coriander leaves - ½ Katori 

* Water - 1 cup


Directions:

Heat the water in a vessel or a teapot until it boils. Roughly chop the coriander leaves and add them to the boiling water. Add the cardamom pieces to the water as well. Let it cold until it reaches a lukewarm temperature so that the coriander leaves can infuse in properly. Filter the mixture and your tea is ready to be consumed.

Hibiscus or the shoe flower In the past, was used in folk medicine.


It was used to treat high blood pressure, liver disease, and fever.


This flower has high amounts of antioxidants.


Women with PCOS are at higher risk for things like type 2 diabetes and metabolic syndrome. 


Studies show that hibiscus can help to improve metabolic markers like insulin, cholesterol and even blood pressure.


Recipe: 

Take fresh or dried hibiscus petals, put in a pinch of spearmint leaves, boil it & consume. 


Ideal time: early morning & early evening

data.title: summer cooler ( Tulsi Moringa Herbal Mix from Satopradhan)
data.description:
Comment “Ideal Time” to know the best time to have it.


This iced tea isn’t just a summer cooler—it’s basically a glow potion in disguise ✨🍃


As a nutritionist, I always say: glowing skin and healthy hair don’t start with what you put on your skin, but with what you put in your body. And this little drink? It’s one of my absolute favorite skin glow rituals, especially when the weather heats up.


I call it my *Skin Glow Iced Tea* 🌿


It’s super simple and so effective: I use the Tulsi Moringa Herbal Mix from @Satopradhan.store (it’s clean, potent, and packed with antioxidants), add a squeeze of fresh lime, a spoon of soaked chia seeds, and pour it all over ice. That’s it!


💚 It helps reduce inflammation

💚 Supports collagen production

💚 Clears up the skin and boosts hair health

💚 Aids in weight balance

💚 And it’s super hydrating and delicious


Just mix half a teaspoon of the herbal tea in warm water, stir in the lime and chia, pour it over ice—and enjoy! It’s honestly my go-to for a refreshing glow boost from the inside out.


Because real beauty starts within... and yes, it can taste this good 🌞

.

data.title: Right Way to Eat Watermelon for Weight Loss!
data.description:
Watermelon: Weight Loss Friendly Hai Ya Nahi?


As a nutritionist, I recommend having watermelon for fat loss this way. (especially for insulin resistance / PCOS):


✅ Always eat the fruit, not the juice

Juice = sugar spike

Fruit = fibre + hydration + satiety


✅ Add chia seeds

Helps slow down sugar release and keeps you fuller for longer.

Bonus: fibre + omega-3 boost!


✅ Lime + mint

Naturally refreshing 💚

Plus lime helps with better blood sugar control + iron absorption from melon.


This isn’t just a fancy detox water — it’s a smart way to enjoy watermelon without the sugar crash!

In India, we don’t yet have the sugar-free, 0-calorie Vanilla Sweet Cream, but if you’re traveling abroad, do ask for it.


This option is safe for those looking to lose weight, people with controlled diabetes, as well as those managing uric acid and thyroid issues.
